title: axios.create returning warp function instead of axios Instance
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the issue
I am trying to create a instance of axios with custom Authorization token. When I try to create the instance it's giving a wrap function instead of axios instance

#### Example Code
Code snippet to illustrate your question

```js
// .... extra code

const apiInstance: AxiosInstance = axios.create({
        headers: {
             Authorization: `${token.token_type} ${token.access_token}`,
        },
});

console.log(apiInstance())

// the output
ƒ wrap() {
        var args = new Array(arguments.length);
        for (var i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
          args[i] = arguments[i];
        }
        return fn.apply(thisArg, args);
}
```
#### Expected behavior, if applicable
axios.create should return a axios instance not wrap function.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.24.0]
 - Adapter [e.g. XHR/HTTP]
 - Browser [Brave]
 - Browser Version [e.g. 22]
 - Node.js Version [14.15.1]
 - OS: [Linux Ubuntu 20.04] 
 - Additional Library Versions [React 17.02]

#### Additional context/Screenshots
Add any other context about the problem here. If applicable, add screenshots to help explain.

#### Use case

I am using a custom hook which after the user logins, it sets a axios Instance with access token obtained after authorization. 

Any help would be appreciated. Thanks in advance :)
author: sleepingsaint

title: Since update from 0.21.4 getting "Invalid Payload: Error: Request failed with status code 400"
body:
#### Describe the bug

Since I updated from 0.21.4 I am getting rejections from my UniFi server.

As an axios client, I receive `Invalid Payload: Error: Request failed with status code 400`
On the server, I see `[https] 4ms 404 POST /auth/login SyntaxError: Unexpected end of JSON input`

I have compared 0.21.4 request log to 0.24.0 and nothing has changed.

#### To Reproduce

To reproduce you need UDM machine and node-red node installed https://github.com/NRCHKB/node-red-contrib-unifi-os/tree/0.5.3

```json

        common: {
            Accept: 'application/json, text/plain, */*'
        },
        delete: {},

            'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'

        put: {
            'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
        },
        patch: {
            'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
        },
        'Content-Length': 15
    },

    ],
```

title: The proxy is not working.
body:
#### Describe the bug
The proxy is not working.

#### To Reproduce
- Add proxy configuration to environment variables:
  ``` sh
  http_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:1080
  https_proxy=http://127.0.0.1:1080
  ```
- Verify that the configuration can be used normally:
  ``` sh
  C:\>http --body https://www.httpbin.org/get
  {
      "args": {},
      "headers": {
          "Accept": "*/*",
          "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate",
          "Host": "www.httpbin.org",
          "User-Agent": "HTTPie/2.4.0",
          "X-Amzn-Trace-Id": "Root=1-61d3f98e-09f187274f147f9d37c5fd15"
      },
      "origin": "*.*.*.*",
      "url": "https://www.httpbin.org/get"
  }
  # *.*.*.* Is already the ip of the proxy server
  ```
- npm i axios@0.24.0
- Create a t.js file with the following content:
  ```js
  const axios = require(`axios`)
  ;!(async () => {
    const httpData = await axios.get(`https://httpbin.org/get`)
    console.log(`httpData`, httpData.data) // The response is very slow, and eventually if it is empty''
    // Check httpData.request.agent.sockets there is an object of 127.0.0.1:1080:
  })();
  ```
- Run t.js: `node t.js`
  ``` sh
  node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())" && node t.js && node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())"
  ```
- Output result: It can be found that the request has passed a long time and the response result is empty.
  ``` txt
  D:\git2\mockm>node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())" && node t.js && node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())"

  2022-01-04T07:24:12.324Z
  httpData 
  2022-01-04T07:25:18.061Z
  ```
  ![](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/filess/img3@main/2022/01/04/1641281210006-126067ae-0dba-4089-a3d3-a9577e5146fc.png)



#### Expected behavior
You can get the response content of the interface.

``` sh
D:\git2\mockm>node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())" && node t.js && node -e "console.log(new Date().toISOString())"
2022-01-04T07:35:18.963Z
httpData {
  args: {},
  headers: {
    Accept: 'application/json, text/plain, */*',
    Host: 'httpbin.org',
    'User-Agent': 'axios/0.24.0',
    'X-Amzn-Trace-Id': 'Root=1-61d3f8b8-54eeb7fd308e15ab414f5441'
  },
  origin: '*.*.*.*',
  url: 'https://httpbin.org/get'
}
2022-01-04T07:35:20.229Z

D:\git2\mockm>
```

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.24.0
 - Adapter HTTP/HTTPS
 - Node.js Version v14.15.5
 - OS: windows10 x64
 - Additional Library Versions [e.g. React 16.7, React Native 0.58.0]

#### Additional context/Screenshots
This may be related to this question:
- https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/925

Proxy service establishment:
Server linux: https://github.com/shadowsocks/go-shadowsocks2/releases/tag/v0.1.5
Client windows: https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-windows/releases/tag/4.4.0.0

title: how to enable typescript intellisense without commonjs and webpack?
body:
#### Describe the issue
I want to use axios WITHOUT webpack and commonjs, to enable typescript intellisense:

1. `npm install --save axios`
2. add a special comment line `/// <reference types="axios" />` in ts file
3. use CDN in html

#### Example Code

index.html:

```
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/axios/dist/axios.min.js"></script>
```

main.ts:

```ts
/// <reference types="axios" />

...
axios.get("/api/infos").then(function (resp: any) {
        console.log(resp);
    }).catch(function (error: any) {
        console.log(error);
    });

```

#### Expected behavior, if applicable
for other libs (I tried babylonjs in the same way), intellisense works fine, but IDE can not find axios definitions.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.24.0]
 - Browser [Chrome]
 - Browser Version [96.0.4664.110]
 - Node.js Version [16.13.1]
 - OS: [Windows10]
 - Additional Library Versions [Typescript 4.5.4, PyCharm 2021.3.1 professional]

#### Additional context/Screenshots
I know webpack works fine for typescript intellisense, but I just want to:

1. save my VPS bandwidth, so use CDN whenever possible
2. this is a demo/training webapp so I need to build everything from scratch, keep things just as simple as possible

title: 403 error at a specific URL
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
I used axios for HTML parsing in my project.
Axios receives a 403 response from a specific URL.
For example, https://www.coupang.com/

I found a solution by comparing [node-fetch](https://www.npmjs.com/package/node-fetch) and axios.
The solution is to add Accept-Encoding to the header.

#### To Reproduce


```js
const axios = require("axios");

const url= "https://www.coupang.com/";

axios.get(url).then((res) => {
  console.log('no Accept-Encoding status:', res.status);
  })
  .catch((error) => {
    console.error('no Accept-Encoding status:', error.response.status);
  });

const config = {
  headers: {
    'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate, br'
  }
};

axios.get(url, config).then((res) => {
  console.log('Accept-Encoding status:', res.status);
})
.catch((error) => {
  console.error('Accept-Encoding status:', error.response.status);
});
```

#### Expected behavior
If you make a request without Accept-Encoding, you will receive a 403 response.
However, if you request by adding Accept-Encoding, you will receive 200 responses.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version ^0.24.0
 - Adapter HTTP/HTTPS
 - Node.js Version v14.17.3
 - OS: windows10 x64

#### Additional context/Screenshots
![axios result](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67548567/148669205-0f15dd8a-3721-46f2-8174-c3bb05818a08.JPG)

title: Why interceptors don't work for axios class but instance?
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the issue
According to the doc, interceptors can be set to axios class or instance.
Actually It won't work for class but instance.

#### Example Code

```js
// WON'T work
axios.interceptors.request.use(config => {
  return config
})

axios.interceptors.response.use(response => {
  return response.data
}, error => {
  return Promise.reject(error)
})

// works
const instance = axios.create({...})

instance.interceptors.request.use(...)
instance.interceptors.response.use(..., ...)
```

#### Expected behavior, if applicable
Works properly.

#### Environment
- Axios Version [0.24.0]
- Adapter [XHR/HTTP]
- Browser [Chrome]
- Browser Version [96.0.4664.55]
- Node.js Version [14.16.1]
- OS: [OSX 10.15.7]
- Additional Library Versions [Vue 3.2.25, Vite 2.7.2]

#### Additional context/Screenshots

title: CVE-2022-0155: Cookie header not cleared upon cross-domain redirect
body:
#### Describe the bug
Axios depends on follow-redirects version 1.14.4.

There's a CVE that allows cookie exposure through redirects (described [here](https://huntr.dev/bounties/fc524e4b-ebb6-427d-ab67-a64181020406/)). This CVE was fixed in follow-redirects v1.14.17.

#### To Reproduce
Run a vulnerability scan

#### Expected behavior
Depend on follow-redirects v1.14.17 or later.

#### Environment

#### Additional context/Screenshots
Related: follow-redirects/follow-redirects#183

body:

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.

- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the issue
Adding a custom serializeDate function isn't working

#### Example Code
I need to change the way date objects are serialized for transmission. I implemented a custom serializer based on https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/1548, but it has no effect. I have verified that the config is being modified, but when I look at the request payload, the dates are still using UTC (2022-02-03T04:59:00.000Z).

Note: My logic is more complicated, I am using toLocalString() in the code below to simplify the example.

```js
const a = axios.create()
a.interceptors.request.use((config) => {
  config.paramsSerializer = (params) =>
    qs.stringify(params, {
      serializeDate: (date: Date) => date.toLocaleString(),
    })
  return config
})
```

#### Expected behavior, if applicable
The data transmitted in the payload match the format I am providing.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.21.1
 - Adapter XHR
 - Browser Chrome
 - Browser Version latest
 - Node.js Version 15.14.0
 - OS: iOS 12.1.0
 - Additional Library Versions Vue 2.6.12

#### Additional context/Screenshots
None

title: Migration to webpack5
body:
#### Describe the bug

Please update your package to migrate to webpack5 as maintaining it in the Debian OS environment caused a bug as we try to build the package with webpack 5.65.0, as you still support of webpack4.44.2.
here is a link to the bug report https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1001392

#### To Reproduce

The failing build output looks like this while building, compiling with webpack 5.65.0

   ```
debian/rules override_dh_auto_build
make[1]: Entering directory '/home/ayoyimika/debian-js-packaging/axios/node-axios'
grunt build
Running "clean:dist" (clean) task
>> 0 paths cleaned.

Running "webpack" task
10% building 0/1 entries 0/0 dependencies 0/0 modules(node:19516) [DEP_WEBPACK_COMPILATION_OPTIMIZE_CHUNK_ASSETS] DeprecationWarning: optimizeChunkAssets is deprecated (use Compilation.hooks.processAssets instead and use one of Compilation.PROCESS_ASSETS_STAGE_* as stage option)
92% sealing asset processing UglifyJSPlugin(node:19516) [DEP_WEBPACK_DEPRECATION_ARRAY_TO_SET] DeprecationWarning: Compilation.chunks was changed from Array to Set (using Array method 'reduce' is deprecated)
(node:19516) [DEP_WEBPACK_DEPRECATION_ARRAY_TO_SET_LENGTH] DeprecationWarning: chunk.files was changed from Array to Set (using Array property 'length' is deprecated)
(node:19516) [DEP_WEBPACK_DEPRECATION_ARRAY_TO_SET_INDEXER] DeprecationWarning: chunk.files was changed from Array to Set (indexing Array is deprecated)
99% cache begin idleasset axios.js 99 bytes [emitted] (name: main)

ERROR in main
Module not found: Error: Recursion in resolving
Stack:
  undefined: (/home/ayoyimika/debian-js-packaging/axios/node-axios) ./index.js

webpack 5.65.0 compiled with 1 error in 446 ms
Warning:  Use --force to continue.

Aborted due to warnings.
make[1]: *** [debian/rules:11: override_dh_auto_build] Error 3
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/ayoyimika/debian-js-packaging/axios/node-axios'
make: *** [debian/rules:8: binary] Error 2
dpkg-buildpackage: error: debian/rules binary subprocess returned exit status 2

```

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.24.0]
 - Adapter [none]
 - Browser [none]
 - Browser Version [none]
 - Node.js Version [ 12.22.7]
 - OS: [Debian 11]
 - Additional Library Versions [webpack5.65.0]

title: Cannot upload file within FormData
body:
After upgrading from 0.24.0 to 0.25.0 Form upload breaks:

#### Describe the bug
A clear and concise description of what the bug is. **If your problem is not a bug, please file under Support or Usage Question**

#### To Reproduce
Original working on 0.24.0 code.
```js
    axios({
        method: "post",
        url: [serverUrl],
        data: formData, // FormData object instance with uploading file field
        timeout: 120000
    });
```
This code was working previously, but now backend cannot extract file field.
After specifying content type:
```js
    axios({
        method: "post",
        url: [serverUrl],
        data: formData, // FormData object instance with uploading file field
        timeout: 120000,
        headers: {"Content-Type": "multipart/form-data"},
    });
``` 
I got an error on backend "bad content-type header, no multipart boundary"

#### Expected behavior
Seems like previous version of axios (0.24.0) automatically add correct header and also boundary.
Or was there any breaking change that I didn't notice?

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.25.0]
 - Node.js Version [16.0.0]
 - Additional Library Versions [formidable 2.0.1]

#### Additional context/Screenshots
Not needed.

title: Timeout setting: invalid when the setting exceeds 60 seconds
body:
When it is set within 60 seconds, everything will execute normally.

When the setting exceeds 60 seconds, such as 80 seconds and 100 seconds, the timeout will always default to 60 seconds.

Hope it can be repaired, thank you!

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [e.g. 0.18.0]
 - Adapter [e.g. XHR/HTTP]
 - Browser [e.g. Chrome]
 - Browser Version [e.g. 94.0.4606.81（正式版本） （32 位）]
 - Node.js Version [e.g. 16.13.0]
 - OS: [e.g. iOS 12.1.0, OSX 10.13.4]

author: loongcaser

title: The setting is invalid when it exceeds 60 seconds
body:
Describe the bug
The setting time exceeds 60 seconds and is invalid
When it is set within 60 seconds, everything will execute normally.
When the setting exceeds 60 seconds, such as 80 seconds and 100 seconds, the timeout will always default to 60 seconds.

To Reproduce

import axios from 'axios'
const httpClient = axios.create()
httpClient.defaults.timeout = 80000

httpClient({
  url: url,
  data: data,
  method: 'post',
  crossOrigin: true,
  dataType: 'json',
  withCredentials: true,
  async: true,
  responseType: 'blob',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf8'
  }
}).then(resp => {
  return resp.data
})

When it exceeds 60 seconds, it will be automatically disconnected and an error will be reported: timeout.
TypeError: net::ERR_FAILED 504


Environment
Axios Version [e.g. 0.18.0]
Adapter [e.g. XHR/HTTP]
Browser [e.g. Chrome]
Browser Version [e.g. 94.0.4606.81（正式版本） （32 位）]
Node.js Version [e.g. 16.13.0]
OS: [e.g. iOS 12.1.0, OSX 10.13.4]
Additional Library Versions [e.g. React 16.7, React Native 0.58.0]

title: The setting is invalid when it exceeds 60 seconds
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
The setting time exceeds 60 seconds and is invalid
When it is set within 60 seconds, everything will execute normally.
When the setting exceeds 60 seconds, such as 80 seconds and 100 seconds, the timeout will always default to 60 seconds.

#### To Reproduce

```js
import axios from 'axios'
const httpClient = axios.create()
httpClient.defaults.timeout = 80000
httpClient({
    url: url,
    data: data,
    method: 'post',
    crossOrigin: true,
    dataType: 'json',
    withCredentials: true,
    async: true,
    responseType: 'blob',
    headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf8'
    }
}).then(resp => {
    return resp.data
})
```

#### Expected behavior
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [e.g. 0.18.0]
 - Adapter [e.g. XHR/HTTP]
 - Browser [e.g. Chrome]
 - Browser Version [e.g. 94]
 - Node.js Version [e.g. 16.13.0]
 - OS: [e.g. iOS 12.1.0, OSX 10.13.4]
 - Additional Library Versions [e.g. React 16.7, React Native 0.58.0]

#### Additional context/Screenshots
Add any other context about the problem here. If applicable, add screenshots to help explain.

```js
import axios from 'axios'
import router from '../router'

const httpClient = axios.create()
httpClient.defaults.timeout = 80000

const call = (method, data, form) => {
  let url = process.env.BASE_API + method
  if (form === 'get') {
    return httpClient.get(url, {params: {...data}}, {
      dataType: 'json',
      withCredentials: true,
      async: true,
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf8'
      }
    }).then(resp => {
      return resp
    })
  } else if (form === 'blob') {
    return httpClient({
      url: url,
      data: data,
      method: 'post',
      crossOrigin: true,
      dataType: 'json',
      withCredentials: true,
      async: true,
      responseType: 'blob',
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf8'
      }
    }).then(resp => {
      return resp.data
    }).catch((err) => {

    })
  } else {
    return httpClient.post(url, data, {
      dataType: 'json',
      withCredentials: true,
      async: true,
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': form === 'form' ? 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' : 'application/json;charset=utf8'
      }
    }).then(resp => {
      return resp
    })
  }
}

httpClient.interceptors.request.use(
  config => {
    if (localStorage.getItem('token')) {
      config.headers['token'] = `${localStorage.getItem('token')}`
    }
    return config
  },
  err => {
    return Promise.reject(err)
  }
)

httpClient.interceptors.response.use(
  response => {
    if (response.data.optCode === '000' && (response.data.message.respCode === '10313'||response.data.message.respCode === '10314')) {
      localStorage.clear()
      router.push({path: '/login'})
    } else if (response.data.optCode === '000' && response.data.message.respCode === '10209') {
      localStorage.clear()
      router.push({path: '/login'})
    }
    return response
  },
  err => {
    return Promise.reject(err)
  }
)

export const api = {
  exportAreaStatis (data) {
    return call('admin/statis/exportAreaStatis', data, 'blob')
  },
}
```

author: TomCorvus

title: Some use-cases may require allowing an empty URL
body:
#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I'd like to give some feedback for the axios 0.25 release. #3791 introduces a fast failure when an empty string is provided as a URL to the request builder. However, my own and at least [one other project](https://github.com/ioBroker/ioBroker.javascript/issues/944) are intentionally using empty strings as URLs and the 0.25 release breaks working code. It _is_ listed as a breaking change in the changelog, but the behavior may be unpractical for some use-cases: Let's imagine a service abstraction over a RESTful resource; the `baseURL` could be something like `http://example.com/examples`, avoiding passing `/examples` as a path with each request.  A `GET` or `POST` request using `""` as a URL would be valid in this case. 

#### Describe the solution you'd like
- Is it possible for the request builder to accept empty strings but fail for undefined?
- Or maybe some config option could be introduced to intentionally opt out of the fast failure.
- An obscure solution would be to additionally pass the `baseURL` as `url` to `axios.create`, but currently the presence of the URL is checked _before_ merging the configs. Moving the [`if (!config.url) {`](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/1163588aa288160282866057efcaef57dbbe417b/lib/core/Axios.js#L39:L43) part down after the config merge would allow this escape hatch.

I'd be happy to submit a PR if desired.

#### Describe alternatives you've considered
- Changing the respective occurrences in my code.
- Using a slash or a space as a URL would work in many cases but it would also introduce trailing slashes.

#### Additional context

title: Cookies not getting set for GET requests made in chrome incognito tab
body: #### Describe the bug When I make a GET request to an API in incognito mode on Google chrome, it does not attach my Auth cookies as part of the request, even though I set the withCredentials option to true. It works perfectly fine on a Normal browser window, and on Firefox's private window. But not in Chrome's incognito mode #### To Reproduce Open Chrome incognito mode Send an axios get request to an auth protected API as such: ```js let cookie = new Cookies(); const user_data = cookie.get("user_data"); const userid = user_data ? user_data.userid : null; const csrf = user_data ? user_data.csrf_token : null; await axios .get( `${process.env.REACT_APP_API_URL}/api/v1/user/progress/?_format=json&user_id=${userid}`, { withCredentials: true, headers: { Accept: "application/hal+json", "Content-Type": "application/hal+json", "X-CSRF-Token": csrf, Authorization: "Basic", }, } ).then(response=>{ console.log(response) } ``` You will observe that your Cookie, will not be attached to the request headers. Trying the same thing on a normal browser tab, will however work. Also trying it with a different request library like fetch, works. #### Expected behaviour I would expect the Cookies to be sent as part of my Request headers As shown in the screenshot below However what happens is shown below, i.e the cookie is not sent as part of the request headers: #### Environment - Axios Version 0.22.0 - Chrome incognito window - Browser Version 96.0.4664.110 - Node.js Version 14.15.0 - OS: macOS Monterey 12.0.1 - React 16.14.0

title: [Feature Request] Support RFC 7230 and RFC 3986 by Axios configuration
body: #### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. Tomcat 7.0.73 add the [RFC 7230](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc7230.txt) and [RFC 3986](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3986.txt) to resolve the HTTP path. But RFC 3986 add the reserved characters in server side, just like ":", "[" and "]". If axios use the path with the query like ``` http://localhost:3000/api/blog?search_title=[Feature Request]balabala ``` The http request will be broken. I search the axios source code, and then found [/lib/helpers/buildURL.js( function encode(val) )](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L11) do this work. #### Describe the solution you'd like I think `config.paramsSerializer` is a good idea to resolve the special words. But in this way, I should rewrite the `paramsSerializer` function in business code. Copy and paste the axios buildURL.js code, and set the `config.paramsSerializer` in global configuration. In my opinion, axios should support RFCs and function `paramsSerializer` doesn't seem very graceful. So can we add a switcher to enable or disable encoding all reserved characters? And let any server, which using RFC-7230 and RFC-3986, work well? Just like these code below. https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L41 ```javascript // configuraion.RFC7230_RFC3986 = true; (maybe another better name) if (!RFC7230_RFC3986) { if (utils.isArray(val)) { key = key + '[]'; } else { val = [val]; } utils.forEach(val, function parseValue(v) { if (utils.isDate(v)) { v = v.toISOString(); } else if (utils.isObject(v)) { v = JSON.stringify(v); } parts.push(encode(key) + '=' + encode(v)); }); } else { if (utils.isArray(val)) { val = val.join(','); } else if (utils.isDate(val)) { val = val.toISOString(); } else if (utils.isObject(val)) { val = JSON.stringify(val); } parts.push(encodeURIComponent(key) + '=' + encodeURIComponent(val)) } ``` #### Describe alternatives you've considered A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered. #### Additional context If you think the solution is fine, I will commit this feature for axios. 😄

```js
const axios = require('axios');

axios.get('/user?ID=12345')
```

title: Error Interceptor - Typing
body:
#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
It is currently no possible to use implicit error type inheritence for interceptors.

#### Describe the solution you'd like
Extend `index.d.ts` (typescript declaration file) request & response interceptor error function signature with the `AxiosError` type.
The function argument type should be a union type, because we can not gurantee, that an interceptor in the chain returns an `AxiosError`.

Mabye provide a utility function, to check whether the error argument is of type `AxiosError` ( duck typing ? ).
E.g `isAxiosError(err?: any) : err is AxiosError`

#### Describe alternatives you've considered
none.

#### Additional context
...

I can create a PR for this, if you want.

title: Circular dependency detected
body:
#### Describe the bug
Circular dependency detected between lib/adapters/xhr.js --> lib/defaults.js and lib/defaults.js -->  lib/adapters/xhr.js


#### To Reproduce
How I fix that problem

in **lib/adapters/xhr.js** 

```js   
var transitional = config.transitional || defaults.transitional
```

change on 

```js
   var transitional = config.transitional || {
        silentJSONParsing: true,
        forcedJSONParsing: true,
        clarifyTimeoutError: false
      };
```
and remove in **lib/adapters/xhr.js** 
```js
 var defaults = require('../defaults');
```

title: An import cycle has been introduced in the 0.20.0
body:
#### Describe the bug

It's a warning, not an actual bug. An import cycle has been introduced in the 0.20.0.  The cycle is `lib/defaults.js` -> `adapters/xhr.js` -> `lib/defaults.js`. This isn't a problem at this moment because by the time the `xhr` module needs the `defaults`, the `defaults` is done exporting. However, be careful and don't change 

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/73e3bdb8835ba942096b662e9441f1d85ce4d484/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L11

to 

```js
var { transitional } = require('../defaults');
```

or you might end up with the uninitialized `transitional` as it's described in https://nodejs.org/dist/latest-v16.x/docs/api/all.html#all_modules_cycles.

I spotted this cycle because I use a [fork](https://github.com/vbraun/circular-dependency-plugin) of  https://github.com/aackerman/circular-dependency-plugin in my project. The thing about that fork is that it works much faster than the original as long as the project doesn't have any import cycles at all, even in the ignored folders.

#### To Reproduce

Again this's not a bug, so I'll just leave permalinks showing where the cycle begins. 

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/73e3bdb8835ba942096b662e9441f1d85ce4d484/lib/defaults.js#L45-L53

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/73e3bdb8835ba942096b662e9441f1d85ce4d484/lib/defaults.js#L18-L28

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/73e3bdb8835ba942096b662e9441f1d85ce4d484/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L11

#### Expected behavior

I suggest untangling by introducing a `lib/default-transitional.js` and importing that module into the `xhr` and `defaults`. Another way to avoid future problems is to add a warning to the [`xhr`](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/73e3bdb8835ba942096b662e9441f1d85ce4d484/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L11) explaining that the `defaults` can't be used at the import time there.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version >= 0.20.0

#### Additional context/Screenshots

Thanks for the lib!

title: Solving Problem: Automatic droping Cookie header across domains
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the issue
After updating library `follow-redirects` due to security (https://github.com/follow-redirects/follow-redirects/commit/8b347cbcef7c7b72a6e9be20f5710c17d6163c22 ) in some of my projects, bugs have appeared related to sending cookies to headers due to redirection from https to http and back. As a result, this security update removes `Cookie` to the final request (although, personally, I didn't need it)

#### Example Code
Sample Cookie Code

```js
axios.post(
    'https://**redirectable to http url**',
    { password, login },
    {  headers: { Cookie: `PHPSESSID=${PHPSESSID}` }, },
);
```

As a result, the `Cookie` will be deleted after the redirect

#### Expected behavior, if applicable
I would like to be able to control the behavior of the `follow-redirects` library by passing `beforeRedirect`.
[An example from a test](https://github.com/follow-redirects/follow-redirects/blob/3d81dc3237b4ffe8b722bb3d1c70a7866657166e/test/test.js#L1537) to solve my problem, but it is unreachable due to the unavailability of `beforeRedirect`

```js
opts.beforeRedirect = function (options) {
  options.protocol = "https:";
};
```

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [^0.20.0]

#### Additional context/Screenshots
### Temporary solution to the problem
Add to `package.json` resolutions for `follow-redirects` with version no higher than `1.14.6`
```json
"resolutions": {
  "axios/follow-redirects": "1.14.6"
},
```

title: Add a discussion tab
body:
## Current situation 
All communication in the axios repo is done via the Issues tab. While great for bug reports and feature suggestions, I find that a discussion board is more suitable for non official discussions.

## Suggestion 
Adding a discussion tab. 
A discussion tab can provide the following benefits: 
- Create a forum in which users can ask questions, and discuss usage of the library
- Create a forum in which contributors discuss the future of the library, and consult about Axios internal code.
- Reduce clutter in the Issues tab.

title: ToFormData - Which way should we proceed?
body:
https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/3757 added a function converts a JS object to a FormData format. 
My suggestion was, to export it independently for now, and proceed in a risk free approach. 
@DigitalBrainJS thought we should add it by default if data is not in FormData format, and content-type is `multipart/form-data`. @DigitalBrainJS merged https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/4413 accordingly. 

Recent `FormData` issues (such as https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/4406) show that tests related to `FormData` are lacking. . If we do decide to publish https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/4413 we need to make sure that there are proper tests for using `toFormData` by default. More specifically, that current usage, passing data in `FormData` format, is not affected at all by the change. 

@jasonsaayman

title: Custom accept header value being ignored on PUT
body:
#### Describe the bug
I am trying to update a record in the [Black Duck REST API](https://ivanti.app.blackduck.com/api-doc/public.html#_updating_a_project_version) that requires the use of a PUT with custom accept and content-type headers. I have checked the payload and headers using postman.

Using Axios (inside an Azure DevOps Pipeline Task), the call I make is 

```
var res = await axiosclient.put(
     theUrl,
     payload,
     {
            headers: {
              'Content-Type': "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
              Accept: "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
              authorization: `Bearer ${token}`
            }
     });
```
This fails with a 406 'Cannot generate acceptable response content'.

On looking at the error logs I see that the `accept` header used does not match what I have set

```
headers:
      { Accept: 'application/json',
        'Content-Type': 'application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json',
        authorization:
         'Bearer <the token>',
        'User-Agent': 'axios/0.26.0',
        'Content-Length': 3536 }
```

I thought it might be the put alias, but get the same problem calling

```
axios({
            method: 'PUT',
            url: theUrl,
            data: payload,
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
                Accept: "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
                authorization: `Bearer ${token}`
            }
        })
```

This problem only seems to occur when the call is made within an Azure DevOps Pipeline Node10 based task. If I run the same logic in a simple script (using Node 16) it works as expected.

Any suggestions why the header value it set is being ignored

#### Expected behavior
Put should honor the `accept` header set as a option in all cases

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.20.0 and 0.26.0]
 - Node.js Version [10]
 - Running inside an Azure DevOps Pipeline Task

- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
I am trying to update a record in the [Black Duck REST API](https://ivanti.app.blackduck.com/api-doc/public.html#_updating_a_project_version) that requires the use of a PUT with custom accept and content-type headers.

#### To Reproduce
Using Axios (inside an Azure DevOps Pipeline Task), the call I make is 

```
var res = await axiosclient.put(
     theUrl,
     payload,
     {
            headers: {
              'Content-Type': "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
              Accept: "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
              authorization: `Bearer ${token}`
            }
     });
```
This fails with a 406 'Cannot generate acceptable response content'.

On looking at the error logs I see that the `accept` header used does not match what I have set

```
headers:
      { Accept: 'application/json',
        'Content-Type': 'application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json',
        authorization:
         'Bearer <the token>',
        'User-Agent': 'axios/0.26.0',
        'Content-Length': 3536 }
```

I tried using a generic REST call, as opposed to the `put` method, as shown below, but this also failed with a 406.

```
    await axiosclient({
        method: 'PUT',
        url: theUrl,
        data: payload,
        headers: {
            'Content-Type': "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
            Accept: "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
            authorization: `Bearer ${token}`
        }
    })
```

**Workaround** - is to use `request-promise`, this works as expected

```
let putOptions = {
            method: 'PUT',
            uri: theUrl,
            body: payload,
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
                'Accept': "application/vnd.blackducksoftware.project-detail-4+json",
              },
            auth: {
                'bearer': token
            },
            json: true
        }

rp(putOptions)

```

#### Expected behavior
Put should honor the `accept` header set as an option in all cases

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.20.0 and 0.26.0]
 - Node.js Version [10]
 - Running inside an Azure DevOps Pipeline Task

#### Additional context/Screenshots
I have also tried using the same code, using the `put()` method, in a standalone Typescript file, running against Node 16 on my PC and here it works as expected. I am only able to reproduce the problem when the code is run within an Azure DevOps Pipeline extension.

Node based Azure DevOps Pipeline extensions execute their code using Node 10 (which cannot changed). Could this be part of the problem?
author: rfennell

title: Error.toJSON() should exclude httpsAgent
body:
#### Describe the bug

if the `httpsAgent` property is set, it is not excluded from `error.toJSON()` resulting in a circular reference error

```none
TypeError: Converting circular structure to JSON
    --> starting at object with constructor 'Object'
    |     property 'httpsAgent' -> object with constructor 'Agent'
    |     property 'sockets' -> object with constructor 'Object'
    |     ...
    |     property 'errored' -> object with constructor 'Object'
    --- property 'config' closes the circle
    at JSON.stringify (<anonymous>)
```

#### To Reproduce

```js
const agent = new https.Agent({  
  rejectUnauthorized: false
});

axios.get("some-dodgy-url", {
  httpsAgent: agent
}).catch((err) => {
  console.error(err.toJSON())
})
```

#### Expected behavior

The error object should be serialisable as JSON without throwing circular reference errors.

#### Environment
 - Axios 0.26.0

#### Additional context/Screenshots

See [this StackOverflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/q/71150003/283366)

title: axios.CancelToken.timeout() function
body:
Currently, there are some running bugs with Axios https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/4364 where request freezes after some time and timeout doesn't work at all.
The current workaround is to cancel the request after a specified timeout.

But for this every time, we have to create a timeout manually and have to cancel it once we got the response before the timeout.
Can we expect some workaround for this? something like
token = axios.CancelToken.timeout(ms: number)
where, after ms millisecond this will call cancel function.

Something similar to https://nodejs.org/api/globals.html#static-method-abortsignaltimeoutdelay

- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
I need axios to use cookies on redirects. I'm using [http-cookie-agent](https://www.npmjs.com/package/http-cookie-agent) but it doesn't work when the agent has `keepAlive: true` 3846masa/http-cookie-agent#65. I'm hesitant to use http-cookie-agent because it relies on internal methods from node's request to intercept headers. It seems error prone and likely to break on future versions of node. 

#### Describe the solution you'd like
Add `axios.interceptors.beforeRedirect`. The beforeRedirect interceptor should have access to the previous request response headers (to read Set-Cookies) and the next requests headers (to set Cookies).
follow-redirects/follow-redirects#138


#### Describe alternatives you've considered

 - I could disable axios redirects and manually redirect requests, similar to [fetch-cookie](https://github.com/valeriangalliat/fetch-cookie#cookies-on-redirects) 
 - I may be able to modify http adapter to pass a beforeRedirect option to [follow-redirects](https://www.npmjs.com/package/follow-redirects), tho I don't see an after redirect hook to extend to handle Set-Cookie headers. 

#### Additional context
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

What's going on here? Why is the behavior different per platform?

title: Add backupURL in axios.create
body:
#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Normally, when a request is made a single URL is used, which is expected to always be online.

However, when the URL becomes unavailable some problems may occur if the application has no way of knowing if the URL is still online, which would cause the app to become unavailable until the URL comes back online.

#### Describe the solution you'd like

The solution that could be given is to add a configuration to allow a backup URL, thus avoiding the loss of users while the main api is not available.

Example:
```js
axios.create({
  baseURL: 'https://example.com/api/',
  backupURL: "https://example.com/backup/api/"
});
```
#### Describe alternatives you've considered
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

#### Additional context
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.
author: neyunse

title: Headers should be compared with case ignored
body:
I think [this issue](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/1237) should be revisited. I understand that in the [specification ](https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec4.html#sec4.2) headers are case insensitive, but it doesn't mean axios should lowercase everything. It should be at least an option to have it lowercased, since it is insensitive, why lower case them?

I think this should be changed because the following reasons:

1. We do not always have control of the API servers we are consuming, and sometimes they require custom headers that are treated with case sensitivity on the backend
2. One of the most common conventions is writing headers as Uppercamelcase-With-Hyphens AWS Example:

"CloudFront-Forwarded-Proto": "https",
"CloudFront-Is-Desktop-Viewer": "true",
"CloudFront-Is-Mobile-Viewer": "false",
"CloudFront-Is-SmartTV-Viewer": "false",
"CloudFront-Is-Tablet-Viewer": "false"

Thank you.

body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.

- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
Error on reject interceptor on tests using axios-mock-adapter or msw.
Trying to have an Axios class with custom interceptors. but, when I try to test a mock and test the rejected interceptor, it never pass through the rejected response interceptor

It work's on the browser, and if I remove the mocking library (for example: `axios-mock-adapter`) it works. but without mocking the expecting data.


#### To Reproduce


```ts
// my-api.ts
import axios from 'axios';

export class MyApi extends axios.Axios {
  constructor(baseURL: string) {
    super({
      baseURL,
    });

    this.interceptors.response.use(
      (data)=> data,
      (error)=> {
         // Never enters here
          return Promise.reject({
          ...error,
          customError: error.message,
        });
      },
    );
  }
}
```

```ts
// my-api.spec.ts
import type { AxiosInstance } from 'axios';
import MockAdapter from 'axios-mock-adapter';

import { MyApi } from './my-api';

describe('my-api', () => {
  // NOTE: simulate an instance of the api
  const api = new MyApi('http://localhost');
  const apiMock = new MockAdapter(api as AxiosInstance);

  it('should handler request error interceptor', async () => {
    try {
      apiMock.onGet('/fake_data').reply(404);
      await api.request({ method: 'get', url: '/fake_data' });
      
      // NOTE: this should never happen since endpoint fails and enters directly on catch
      expect(1).toBe(2);
    } catch (error) {
      const { customError } = error as { customError: string };
      expect(customError).toBe('example error');
    }
  });
});
```

#### Expected behavior
it should enter into the error interceptor

#### Environment
 - Axios Version [0.26.0]
 - Node.js Version [17.1.0]
 - OS: [MacOS Monterrey 12.1.0]
 - Additional Library Versions [axios-mock-adapter@1.20.0,  jest@27.5.1, ts-jest@27.1.3, msw@0.38.2]

#### To Reproduce

```js
  test('axios request post do carry data', async () => {
    // mock url
    const postUrl = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts'
    const postData = { title: 'foo', body: 'bar', userId: 1 }

    await axios.request({
      method: 'post',
      url: postUrl,
      // timeout error
      // data: postData
    })

    // success
    await axios.post(
      postUrl,
      // timeout error
      // postData
    )

    const getUrl = 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/comments'
    const getQuery = { postId: 1 }
    
    // success
    await axios.get(getUrl, { params: getQuery })
  })
```
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/53166933/158501662-052f08cb-376a-4b9f-bd4c-a439617a9338.png)

#### Expected behavior
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/53166933/158501720-bfd07849-1bd2-4564-9f60-a373fd7ca42e.png)

#### Environment
 - Axios Version: 0.24.0
 - Adapter: HTTP
 - Node.js: Version 16.14.0
 - OS: Windows 10

#### Additional context/Screenshots
Non-get methods, such as put and delete method, will have the same error if they carry data (body)

body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.

- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the issue

A library we are using has axios as dependency.
We are using Angular Universal with Node Express for server side rendering.

Since we added the library having axios as dependency, ssr is not working anymore.

The problem lies in lib/helpers/isURLSameOrigin.js, where we have the errror "Cannot read property 'charAt' of undefined" 
However, as this is running server side, I think it should not perform this anyway, but use nonStandardBrowserEnv function.

In lib/utils.js , the function isStandardBrowserEnv is used to determine the brower env via window.navigator.product, which returns 'Gecko' (via domino), and therefor it assumes it is running in a standard browser.


#### Expected behavior

Any possibility, e.g. via configuration or else, to change the evaluation of isStandardBrowserEnv so that it works in the described environment.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.21.4
 - Node.js Version 14.18.1
 - Node Express 4.15.2
 - domino 2.1.6
author: romankh

title: Proxy support does not work properly with CONNECT on https requests
body:
#### Describe the bug
Using Axios 0.26.1 behind a corporate HTTP tunneling proxy to an HTTPS destination, once the proxy connection is made, a POST method is issued, rather than a CONNECT to establish the TLS tunnel to the destination host where the POST should be sent. Result is **Error: socket hang up**, as the proxy expects a CONNECT to be issued and drops the connection

Specifically, using wireshark to monitor the activity of the script goes as follow:
> TCP SYN
< TCP SYN, ACK
> TCP ACK
> HTTP POST
< TCP FIN, ACK
> TCP ACK
![axios_pcap](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15218042/158659772-3d084e3b-8ab1-4f99-8c4d-0a54ed697ed4.png)

If you use curl with the same environment, the packets are as follows:
> TCP SYN
< TCP SYN, ACK
> TCP ACK
> HTTP CONNECT
< HTTP Connection established
> TCP ACK
> TLS Client Hello
< TLS Server Hello
... encrypted POST and response follows
![curl_pcap](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15218042/158659774-5c84d76a-3370-4acb-9a5d-f9270de12bf7.png)


#### To Reproduce

```bash
export http_proxy="http://user:password@10.123.173.125:80"
export https_proxy="http://user:password@10.123.173.125:80"
```

```js
http.post('https://api.github.com/user/repos', {});
```

#### Expected behavior
When connecting to an HTTPS server through a tunneling HTTP proxy, the CONNECT method should be used to establish the TLS handshake before issuing the POST

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.26.1
 - Adapter HTTP
 - Node.js Version 14.8.0
 - OS: Windows 10 (10.0.19042.1526)
author: gm-ghanover

title: Proxy config via HTTP_PROXY env variable on explicit port 80 doesn't work
body:
#### Describe the bug I'm in company environment behind a proxy, the proxy host is `10.90.176.62` and is configured on the env vars HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY as `http://10.90.176.62:80` **notice the trailing port :80**. Then I'm using Axios on NodeJS (v14) and with some URLs the HTTP call doesn't work, I receive the following error (please find the full log at the end of the post): ``` Error: read ECONNRESET at TLSWrap.onStreamRead (internal/stream_base_commons.js:209:20) at TLSWrap.callbackTrampoline (internal/async_hooks.js:130:17) { errno: -4077, code: 'ECONNRESET', ``` #### To Reproduce I'm trying to call GitHub Enterprise API but I get the `ECONNRESET` error mentioned above. ❌ not working ```js const axios = require('axios') process.env.HTTP_PROXY // > 'http://10.90.176.62:80' axios.get('https://github.tools.digital.mycompany.com/api/v3/repos/LQ6211/portfolio_name_fix').catch(console.log) ``` After some debugging I noticed that if I manually set the same proxy value (`http://10.90.176.62:80`) in `axios.get` options, I receive the same error, so the proxy is correctly set from the `HTTP_PROXY` variable: ❌ not working ``` // This is equivalent to using HTTP_PROXY=http://10.90.176.62:80 and results in an error axios.get('https://github.tools.digital.mycompany.com/api/v3/repos/LQ6211/portfolio_name_fix', { proxy: { protocol: 'http', host: '10.90.176.62', port: 80 } }) ``` ✔ Working Finally I tried to omit the port and magically it works correctly: ``` axios.get('https://github.tools.digital.mycompany.com/api/v3/repos/LQ6211/portfolio_name_fix', { proxy: { protocol: 'http', host: '10.90.176.62', // Remove the port! // port: 80 } }) ``` Then I tried to set HTTP_PROXY env var to `http://10.90.176.62` and it works, so it's definitely the port `80` in the proxy URL. For curiosity, I tried to set 443 as the proxy port (still with the http protocol) as the final URL is on HTTP**S** and it works as well. So something weird is going on. For what is worth, with some URLs the http calls work also when the proxy includes the port `80` in the config. #### Expected behavior I expect the HTTP call to correctly work and correctly set the proxy. #### Environment - Axios Version [from 0.21.4 to 0.26.1] - Adapter [Node] - Browser [N/A] - Browser Version [N/A] - Node.js Version [14.18.0] - OS: [Microsoft Windows 10 Enterprise V 10.0.18363 Build 18363] - Additional Library Versions [N/A] #### Additional context/Screenshots

title: Happy Eyeballs with Axios?
body:
Hi

Is there a way to implement something like Happy Eyeballs (RFC 8305) in Axios?

This means that if a hostname has several A records in its DNS answer, Axios must continue trying IP after IP until one gets a proper reply. 

Currently, Axios only uses the 1st IP from the DNS reply. Is there a way to force it to continue trying other IPs if the first one is not responsive?

Thanks

title: Project status: What target platforms should Axios support and which version of ECMA should be used for PRs and code refactors?
body:
#### Describe the issue
We need to clarify in the Contribution guide which ECMA version and target platforms should be supported, otherwise, we are doomed to have a lot of rudimentary code and inconsistencies. 
Axios is written in a mix of ES3 and ES5 ECMA versions and it currently claims support for IE11 and newer platforms, but recent PRs use ES5+ features that are not supported by IE without any built-in polyfills. For example, we have trim util that has been deprecated since IE10, while we have Array.prototype.includes that IE doesn't support.

#### Example Code
N/A

title: req errors silently ignored when req.aborted = true
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
If the underlying request has an error and `req.aborted = true` then the error and promise are ignored. Running the code below just exits execution without resolving or rejecting the promise.

#### To Reproduce

```js
(async () => {
	const axios = require('axios');
	try {
		console.log('making request');
		const response = await axios({url: 'http://copiersupplystore.com'}); // currently produces the underlying error "Unsupported protocol httsp"
		console.log('response', response);
	} catch (e) {
		console.error('error', e);
	}
})().catch(console.error);
```

#### Expected behavior
The promise should always either resolve or reject. This code in `lib/adapters/http.js` shoudn't ignore the error if `req.aborted = true`
```
    // Handle errors
    req.on('error', function handleRequestError(err) {
      if (req.aborted && err.code !== 'ERR_FR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS') return;
      reject(enhanceError(err, config, null, req));
    });
```

#### Environment
 - Axios Version: 0.26.1
 - Adapter: http
 - Browser: nodejs
 - Node.js Version: 14.18.0
 - OS: Windows 10

title: Cannot get error message (XML) from S3 when uploading an asset over size limit
body: I am using Axios to upload an asset to S3 using signed URLs. `axios: 0.24.0` `node: v14.17.4` It works correctly for files under the upload size limit, but when trying to upload an asset (.tiff, 114 Mb) over the upload size limit (50 Mb), I cannot get the XML error from S3. When I use the **request** library, I get this as the **error** content : ``` UPLOAD FAILED. Status Code: 400. Error message: \nEntityTooLargeYour proposed upload exceeds the maximum allowed size5243278852428800N8RFYCS0K10GB4FF2OaG69rf19DMxItXx8J//rscZuSbxU8hJ/93du/a1zaFwAZa6jN2v1xCDebW4MRMgW3Kfuw0n10= ``` But when I use AXIOS, I get back the error code 400, but no message back: ``` { "message": "Request failed with status code 400", "name": "Error", "stack": "Error: Request failed with status code 400\n at createError (/Users/vlad/modules/node_modules/axios/lib/core/createError.js:16:15)\n at settle (/Users/vlad/modules/axios/lib/core/settle.js:17:12)\n at IncomingMessage.handleStreamEnd (/Users/vlad/modules/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:322:11)\n at IncomingMessage.emit (events.js:412:35)\n at endReadableNT (internal/streams/readable.js:1317:12)\n at processTicksAndRejections (internal/process/task_queues.js:82:21)", "config": { "transitional": { "silentJSONParsing": false, "forcedJSONParsing": false, "clarifyTimeoutError": true }, "transformRequest": [ null ], "transformResponse": [ null ], "timeout": 0, "xsrfCookieName": "XSRF-TOKEN", "xsrfHeaderName": "X-XSRF-TOKEN", "maxContentLength": null, "maxBodyLength": null, "headers": { "Accept": "application/json, text/plain, */*", "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data; boundary=--------------------------913873697821807580301477", "content-length": 114310407, "User-Agent": "axios/0.26.0" }, "method": "post", "url": "https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/public-assets.bluescape.com", "data": { "_overheadLength": 938, "_valueLength": 114309413, "_valuesToMeasure": [], "writable": false, "readable": true, "dataSize": 0, "maxDataSize": 2097152, "pauseStreams": true, "_released": true, "_streams": [], "_currentStream": null, "_insideLoop": false, "_pendingNext": false, "_boundary": "--------------------------913873697821807580301477", "_events": {}, "_eventsCount": 1 }, "axios-retry": { "retryCount": 0, "lastRequestTime": 1648080399442 } }, "status": 400 } ``` Any idea how can I get back the XML from S3 describing the issue? title: TypeError: Failed to execute 'setRequestHeader' on 'XMLHttpRequest': String contains non ISO-8859-1 code point.
body:
#### Describe the issue
When trying to upload a file that has non ISO chars (Eg: **_Những hình dạng của kinh tế hậu Tuổi Trẻ Online.pdf_**), axios throws an error, saying: 

"TypeError: Failed to execute 'setRequestHeader' on 'XMLHttpRequest': String contains non ISO-8859-1 code point."



#### Example Code
Code snippet to illustrate your question

```js
const formData = new FormData();
formData.append("file", selectedFile, file.name);

const response = await axios.post("/upload", formData, {
          headers: {
            "x-file-name":  file.name,
            "x-file-size":  file.size
          }
        });
```

#### Expected behavior, if applicable
While files with plain english names (eg: test.pdf) gets uploaded easily, non ISO file names (mentioned above) throws the above exception.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version : 0.21.1
 - Adapter XHR
 - Browser Chrome
 - Browser Version:  99.0.4844.74
 - Node.js Version v17.1.0
 - OS: OSX 12.0.1
 - Additional Library Versions React 16.13.1

#### Additional context/Screenshots
https://share.getcloudapp.com/YEurQxQN

title: Multipart form parse error - Invalid boundary in multipart: None
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
Axios is not setting content-type automatically as per the data (like fetch, which works fine).

#### To Reproduce
When we have to upload a file, we need to have `Content-Type` set to `multipart/form-data` but, after doing so, axios gives this error;

> - Multipart form parse error - Invalid boundary in multipart: None

But in fetch (also recommended by other in axios too), we don't need to set content-type manually but the package automatically detects it as per the data send.

I am using FormData for files but this is not working anyway.


```js
axios.post(url, data, {headers: 'Content-Type': "multipart/form-data"})
```

And if i remove the content-type header, then this error shows up;

> Unsupported media type \"application/json\" in request.

#### Expected behavior
So how much i know, it shouldn't be a problem but should be handled by axios. Also this happened after i upgraded axios and form-data to latest versions available today.

#### Environment
 - "axios": "^0.26.1",
 -"react": "17.0.1",
 -"react-native": "0.64.3",
 -"form-data": "^4.0.0",

title: timeoutErrorMessage ignored
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Issue
TimeoutErrorMessage ignored. Does not return error message.

#### Code

```js
try {
      if (!Array.isArray(path)) {
        path = [path];
      }
      const url = createUrl(this.restPath, ...path);

      const options: AxiosRequestConfig = {
        url,
        responseType,
        params: queryParams,
        method: 'GET',
        timeout: 1000 * 10,
        timeoutErrorMessage: 'error, not connected', // not working
      };
      return this.request(options);
    } catch (e) {
      // if (timeout) throw new error ('error, not connected');
      error(e);
      throw e;
    }  
```
 #### Axios Config
 
```js
    export interface AxiosRequestConfig<D = any> {
  url?: string;
```

title: optimize some code
body:
The following line of code comes from https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/lib/adapters/http.js#L403:
```js
reject(!cancel || (cancel && cancel.type) ? new CanceledError() : cancel);
```
If `!cancel` is false, it means cancel is not false. So it can be optimized as follows:
```js
reject(!cancel || cancel.type ? new CanceledError() : cancel);
```
author: zh-lx

title: optimazition of code
body:
#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
This is an optimazition of code.

#### Describe the solution you'd like
The following line of code comes from https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/lib/adapters/http.js#L403:
```js
reject(!cancel || (cancel && cancel.type) ? new CanceledError() : cancel);
```
It can be optimized as follows:
```js
reject(!cancel || cancel.type ? new CanceledError() : cancel);
```

#### Describe alternatives you've considered
When the code is executed to `(cancel && cancel.type)`, it means `!cancel` is false, so `cancel` is true, we can get `cancel.type` directly.

#### Additional context
no more

#### Describe the bug
"multipart/form-data" requests throw an exception: 

``` Multipart form parse error - Invalid boundary in multipart: None```

#### To Reproduce
Simple multipart/form-data request:

```js
    const data = new FormData();
    spotInfos.pictures.forEach((file, index) => {
        data.append('pictures', {
            uri: file.path, // your file path string
            name: file.name,
            type: file.type
        })
    });
    spotInfos.tags.forEach(t => data.append('tags', t))
    data.append('name', spotInfos.name);
    data.append('description', spotInfos.description);
    return axiosInstance.post(`spots/`,
        data,
        {
            headers: { 'Accept': 'application/json', 'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data', },
        });
```
This code works on axios 0.24.0

#### Expected behavior
No error

#### Environment
 - Axios 0.25.0 
 - React Native 0.66.4

I have tested the latest version too (0.26.1)

-->

#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.
Not a problem, but there could possibly be a more 'elegant' way of initialising the `args` variable in `lib/helpers/bind.js`.

#### Describe the solution you'd like
The function currently uses a loop to copy elements from the `arguments` object into an `Array`. According to this MDN page about arguments, the above task can be done simply with one line:
`var args = Array.from(arguments);`

#### Describe alternatives you've considered
There are other alternative routes to perform this action but the above suggestion seems to be the most concise and efficient.

#### Additional context
N/A

title: Axios instance not callable from within custom class
body:
<!-- Click "Preview" for a more readable version --

Please read and follow the instructions before submitting an issue:

- Read all our documentation, especially the [README](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/README.md). It may contain information that helps you solve your issue.
- Ensure your issue isn't already [reported](https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue).
- If you aren't sure that the issue is caused by Axios or you just need help, please use [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/axios) or [our chat](https://gitter.im/mzabriskie/axios).
- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest Axios version.
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

⚠️👆 Feel free to these instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️
-->

#### Describe the bug
Axios instance call doesn't return when called from within a custom class. I suspect it's related to the internal `this` keyword.

#### To Reproduce
The custom Axios class:

```js
class Axios {
  #instance: AxiosInstance

  constructor({ baseURL }: Config) {
    this.#instance = axios.create({
      baseURL,
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    })

    this.#setupInterceptors()
  }

...

  async #interceptResponseError(
    error: AxiosError,
  ): Promise<AxiosError | AxiosResponse> {
    if (error.response.status === 401) {
      await refreshAuthorization()

      return await this.#instance(error.config) // This never returns
    }

    return await Promise.reject(error.response)
  }


}

export default Axios
```

#### Expected behavior
I would expect this code to work without errors and if it's necessary to use `this` internally I would somehow make it bound permanently so that it doesn't break when used with classes.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.26.1
 - Adapter default
 - Browser IOS Simulator
 - Browser Version -
 - Node.js Version 16.5.0
 - OS: -
 - Additional Library Versions: React 17.0.1, React Native 0.64.3

#### Additional context/Screenshots

I've confirmed that it's not a problem with my code by moving the const instance = axios.create outside of class and using this variable instead of this.#instance. It does make the code work, but it creates a monolith which I wanted to avoid.

```js
    const headers: AxiosResponseHeaders = {
      'set-cookie': ['JSESSIONID=test']

      status: 0,
```

title: Support formdata-node
body:
`got` [recommends](https://github.com/sindresorhus/got/blob/e3936629ce8b075749f33260b8cf036c7832e448/documentation/2-options.md#body) using [`formdata-node`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/formdata-node). Axios [recommends](https://axios-http.com/docs/urlencoded#form-data) [`form-data`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/form-data).

I would like to also use `formdata-node` with both. I want use both `got` and Axios and I don't want people reading the code to need to notice that there's two different libraries used for `FormData`. Also `formdata-node` has a table saying that `form-data`'s `FormData` doesn't a bunch of methods that the browser's does. I tried using `formdata-node` with Axios

```sh
cd /tmp
mkdir axios-form-test
cd axios-form-test
npm init -y
npm install axios
npm install formdata-node
node
```

```js
const axios = require('axios');
const { FormData } = require('formdata-node');

const form = new FormData();
form.append('my_field', 'my value');
form.append('my_other_field', 'my second value');

axios.post('http://localhost:8888', form)
```

and got an error

```none
Uncaught:
Error: Data after transformation must be a string, an ArrayBuffer, a Buffer, or a Stream
    at createError (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/core/createError.js:16:15)
    at dispatchHttpRequest (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:98:23)
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at httpAdapter (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:48:10)
    at dispatchRequest (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/core/dispatchRequest.js:58:10)
    at Axios.request (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/core/Axios.js:108:15)
    at Axios.<computed> [as post] (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/core/Axios.js:140:17)
    at Function.wrap [as post] (/private/tmp/axios-test/node_modules/axios/lib/helpers/bind.js:9:15) {
  config: { [...]
```

title: axios v0.27.0 uses node polyfills provided by webpack, which are removed from webpack v5
body:
#### Describe the bug
I'm using axios in an app created by create-react-app. As soon I upgraded axios to v0.27.0, the application stop working with the error shown in the reproduction section. Downgrading to v0.26.1 solved the issue.

It looks like the issues rises from the xhr adapter.

#### To Reproduce
Create an empty project using CRA

```shell
npx create-react-app my-app
cd my-app
```

install axios

```shell
npm install axios
```

use axios to make any request

```js
useEffect(() => {
  axios.get("https://google.com");
}, []);
```

start the application

```shell
npm start
```

The error is shown in the console

```shell
Module not found: Error: Can't resolve 'url' in '/home/mayo/personal-projects/my-app/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters'
BREAKING CHANGE: webpack < 5 used to include polyfills for node.js core modules by default.
This is no longer the case. Verify if you need this module and configure a polyfill for it.

If you want to include a polyfill, you need to:
        - add a fallback 'resolve.fallback: { "url": require.resolve("url/") }'
        - install 'url'
If you don't want to include a polyfill, you can use an empty module like this:
        resolve.fallback: { "url": false }
ERROR in ./node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/xhr.js 17:10-24
Module not found: Error: Can't resolve 'url' in '/home/mayo/personal-projects/my-app/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters'

BREAKING CHANGE: webpack < 5 used to include polyfills for node.js core modules by default.
This is no longer the case. Verify if you need this module and configure a polyfill for it.

If you want to include a polyfill, you need to:
        - add a fallback 'resolve.fallback: { "url": require.resolve("url/") }'
        - install 'url'
If you don't want to include a polyfill, you can use an empty module like this:
        resolve.fallback: { "url": false }

webpack compiled with 1 error
```

#### Expected behavior
That it works, or at least there are instruction to install the dependencies in the documentation.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.27.0
 - Additional Library Versions create-react-app (react-scripts) v5

title: Unexpected 'Unsupported protocol' error for relative URLs containing colon (:) character
body:
#### Describe the bug
For some REST API endpoints we use [Google Cloud API Custom Methods](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/custom_methods) URL design like `/api/resource:customVerb`.

These worked with `axios` prior to **0.27.1** but now they result in an error like:

`AxiosError { message: "Unsupported protocol /api/resource:', code: "ERR_BAD_REQUEST", ... }`

#### To Reproduce
Try to have `axios` get a relative URL with a colon in it, e.g.
```js
axios.get("/api/resource:customVerb");
```

#### Environment
 - Axios Version: 0.27.1
 - Browser: Microsoft Edge 100.0.1185.50
 - Node.js Version: 16.14.2
 - OS: Windows 10 Pro

#### Describe the bug

Error on Content-Type and boundary value

Use that api to post formData, Axios has been upgraded to version 0.27.1. As a result, pictures in our production environment cannot be uploaded and used, which should be a big problem!

```js
let formData = new FormData(document.querySelector('form'))
axios
  .post('/login.php', formData)
  .then(response => {

        alert(response.data.msg)
      } else {
        alert(response.data)
      }
  })
    .catch(error => {
    alert(error)
  })
```

#### Expected behavior
Hope it can be repaired soon.

#### Environment
Axios Version 0.27.1
Adapter HTTP
Browser Chrome
Browser Version 100
PHP Version 8.0

title: AxiosError ERR_NETWORK contains request as response
body:
#### Describe the bug
When performing an axios request which is rejected with `ERR_NETWORK` the created `AxiosError` contains the request as response.
This leads to the issue that the response of the `AxiosError` contains the status code 200 and the body of the request although in reality no response was received.

I think the issue is that the request is passed as 5th parameter (response) of the `AxiosError` constructor here:
https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/bc733fec78326609e751187c9d453cee9bf1993a/lib/adapters/xhr.js#L119

#### To Reproduce

Sample project:
[sample.zip](https://github.com/axios/axios/files/8588127/sample.zip)

Running the sample project with `npm run test` prints the following to the console:
```
error code: ERR_NETWORK
response status: 200
response body: {"foo":"bar"}
```

#### Expected behavior
The response field in the `AxiosError` created due to `ERR_NETWORK` should be undefined.

#### Environment
 - Axios Version 0.27.2
 - Adapter XHR
 - Browser -
 - Browser Version -
 - Node.js Version 18.0.0
 - OS: macOS 12.3.1
 - Additional Library Versions msw: ^0.39.2

#### Additional context/Screenshots
 
NA
