title: [1.0.0+] Regression - settle helper not exported
body:
### Describe the bug

When upgrading to v1.0.0+ our test suites fail with the following error:

```
Error [ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED]: Package subpath './lib/core/settle' is not defined by "exports" in .\node_modules\axios\package.json
    at new NodeError (internal/errors.js:322:7)
    at throwExportsNotFound (internal/modules/esm/resolve.js:332:9)
    at packageExportsResolve (internal/modules/esm/resolve.js:565:3)
    at resolveExports (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:450:36)
    at Function.Module._findPath (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:490:31)
    at Function.Module._resolveFilename (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:888:27)
    at Function.Module._resolveFilename.sharedData.moduleResolveFilenameHook.installedValue [as _resolveFilename] (.\node_modules\@cspotcode\source-map-support\source-map-support.js:811:30)
    at Function.Module._load (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:746:27)
    at Module.require (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:974:19)
    at Module.patchedRequire [as require] (.\node_modules\diagnostic-channel\dist\src\patchRequire.js:16:46)
    at require (internal/modules/cjs/helpers.js:101:18)
    at Object.<anonymous> (.\lib\test\test-file.int.ts:13:16)
    at Module._compile (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1085:14)
    at Module.m._compile (.\node_modules\ts-node\src\index.ts:1618:23)
    at Module._extensions..js (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1114:10)
    at Object.require.extensions.<computed> [as .ts] (.\node_modules\ts-node\src\index.ts:1621:12)
    at Module.load (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:950:32)
    at Function.Module._load (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:790:12)
    at Module.require (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:974:19)
    at Module.patchedRequire [as require] (.\node_modules\diagnostic-channel\dist\src\patchRequire.js:16:46)
    at require (internal/modules/cjs/helpers.js:101:18)
    at Object.exports.requireOrImport (.\node_modules\mocha\lib\nodejs\esm-utils.js:49:16)
    at async Object.exports.loadFilesAsync (.\node_modules\mocha\lib\nodejs\esm-utils.js:91:20)
    at async singleRun (.\node_modules\mocha\lib\cli\run-helpers.js:125:3)
    at async Object.exports.handler (.\node_modules\mocha\lib\cli\run.js:370:5)
```

The [request config docs](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.1.3/README.md#request-config) state that the following for the `adapter` prop:

>  // `adapter` allows custom handling of requests which makes testing easier.
>  // Return a promise and supply a valid response (see lib/adapters/README.md).

The [`lib/adapters/README.md`](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.1.3/lib/adapters/README.md) file indicates that the `settle` helper function should be used when authoring a custom adapter. However, when attempting to include the `settle` function (which was possible in v0) the above error is thrown because the `settle` helper is not exported.

### To Reproduce

Following the instructions in the [readme](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.1.3/lib/adapters/README.md) reproduce the  error reliably.

### Code snippet

```js
var settle = require('axios/lib/core/settle');

module.exports = function myAdapter(config) {
  // At this point:
  //  - config has been merged with defaults
  //  - request transformers have already run
  //  - request interceptors have already run
  
  // Make the request using config provided
  // Upon response settle the Promise

  return new Promise(function(resolve, reject) {
  
    var response = {
      data: responseData,
      status: request.status,
      statusText: request.statusText,
      headers: responseHeaders,
      config: config,
      request: request
    };

    settle(resolve, reject, response);

    // From here:
    //  - response transformers will run
    //  - response interceptors will run
  });
}
```


### Expected behavior

The documentation should work as expected. Or `settle` should be exported as a member of the package itself.

### Axios Version

1.0.0

### Adapter Version

n/a

n/a

n/a

all

all

title: `maxContentLength` and `maxBodyLength` ignored when `responseType` is set as `stream`
body:
### Describe the bug

When fetching with `responseType` set as `stream`, `maxContentLength` and `maxBodyLength` are completely ignored.
Even setting those values with very low values, like `1`, the request is executed correctly, fetching even large files.

### To Reproduce

Set  `responseType` to `stream` and fetch a large file

https://runkit.com/lughino/runkit-npm-axios

### Code snippet

```js
// inside an express middleware
(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
  const { url } = req.body

  const stream = await axios(url, {
    responseType: 'stream',
    maxContentLength: 2,
    maxBodyLength: 1,
  })

  stream.data
    .on('error', (err) => {
      res.end('Error on loading')
    })
    .pipe(someTransformation)
    .on('error', (err) => {
      res.end(`Error on processing: ${err}`)
    })
    .pipe(res)
    .on('finish', () => res.end())
}
```


### Expected behavior

To throw an error and interrupting the request

### Axios Version

1.1.3

### Adapter Version

http? the default in node

title: Error response headers not being populated inside OnRejected response interceptor
body:
### Describe the bug

When trying to access the value of a custom header inside an *OnRejected* interceptor for an error reponse, it will always come up undefined. This happens when trying to access it using both `error.response.headers['x-my-header']` and `error.config.headers['x-my-header']`. My chrome network tab does show the header is received, however I cannot access it inside the axios error or the error response. Oddly enough, `content-type` and `content-length` headers are being populated insde `error.response.headers`. Error response headers are being set by an express server in my case. 

### To Reproduce

1. Set header for a response with statusCode 401 as `res.setHeader('x-my-header', 1)
2. Access response headers inside interceptor
``` 
axios.interceptors.response.use(
    (response) => response,
    async (error) => {
      if (
        axios.isAxiosError(error) &&
        error.response
      ) {
        console.log(error.response.headers.['x-my-header']
      }
      throw error;
    }
  );
  ```

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

Custom http headers to be set inside `error.response.headers`

1.1.3

author: Eazash

title: Axios includes br in Accept-Encoding even if transparent decompression is not supported
body:
### Describe the bug

Axios has optional support for transparently decompressing brotli-encoded responses, depending on whether brotli support is available in the runtime.

```js
const isBrotliSupported = utils.isFunction(zlib.createBrotliDecompress);
```

```js
        case 'br':
          if (isBrotliSupported) {
            streams.push(zlib.createBrotliDecompress());
            delete res.headers['content-encoding'];
          }
```

However, it unconditionally includes `br` in the `Accept-Encoding` header it sets (if the header wasn’t specified by the library user), regardless of whether the runtime supports brotli or not.

```js
    headers.set('Accept-Encoding', 'gzip, deflate, br', false);
```

So if brotli support isn’t available, and the server decides to encode the response using brotli (which it’s allowed to, based on the header axios added), then the library user will be left with a response they can’t decode, even though they don’t asked for brotli encoding.

### To Reproduce

_No response_

_No response_

### Expected behavior

_No response_

title: Force axios to use the http adapter instead of xhr
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I am creating a library that will mostly be used in electron projects. The library needs to perform http request to a localhost server that uses a self signed certificate, so I need to use https.Agent. The problem is, that axios uses the xhr adapter instead of the http adapter. When i try to import the correct adapter from `axios/lib/adapters/http` I get the error `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`. 

### Describe the solution you'd like

I need a way to tell axios to always use the http adapter. The easiest way to do this is by exposing `axios/lib/adapters/http`.

title: ECONNRESET at Function.AxiosError.from
body:
Hi there,

I was using axios v0.21.4 and constantly receiving ECONNRESET errors throughout the day:

```
Error: read ECONNRESET
    at TLSWrap.onStreamRead (node:internal/stream_base_commons:217:20)
```

After updating to the latest version (v1.1.3) and some rewrites to use the AbortController instead of CancelToken and an NodeJS-Upgrade (v18) I still receive the EONNRESET error but with some more information:

```
Error: read ECONNRESET
    at Function.AxiosError.from (/home/app/appDir/node_modules/axios/lib/core/AxiosError.js:89:14)
    at RedirectableRequest.handleRequestError (/home/app/appDir/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:516:25)
    at RedirectableRequest.emit (node:events:525:35)
    at ClientRequest.eventHandlers.<computed> (/home/app/appDir/node_modules/follow-redirects/index.js:14:24)
    at ClientRequest.emit (node:events:513:28)
    at TLSSocket.socketErrorListener (node:_http_client:494:9)
    at TLSSocket.emit (node:events:513:28)
    at emitErrorNT (node:internal/streams/destroy:151:8)
    at emitErrorCloseNT (node:internal/streams/destroy:116:3)
    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:82:21)
```

**Further information**

I send similar requests over and over throughout the day.
Some of the requests (axios.get) are handled correctly, some return with

```
AxiosError: timeout of 2000ms exceeded
    at RedirectableRequest.handleRequestTimeout (/home/app/appDir/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:553:16)
    at RedirectableRequest.emit (node:events:513:28)
    at Timeout.<anonymous> (/home/app/appDir/node_modules/follow-redirects/index.js:169:12)
    at listOnTimeout (node:internal/timers:564:17)
    at processTimers (node:internal/timers:507:7)
```
and some lead to the ECONNRESET error above.


Is this a problem within my code or a general problem within axios?

### Axios Version

1.1.3

### Node.js Version

18

title: Setting `content-type` lowercased makes axios prefixing 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' to it
body: ### Describe the bug If you make an axios request with a lower case `content-type` header axios changes the header to `application/x-www-form-urlencoded, your/content-type`. Changing the header to `Content-Type` will make the request work correctly. author: jakguru

title: Axios takes very long time to GET some specific webpage when User-Agent contains browser info
body:
### Describe the bug

- When I'm using axios nodejs sending a GET request to https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/about with `'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/103.0.5060.114'`, it takes about 9s to get the response.
- But if I remove the `'Chrome/103.0.5060.114'` in the User-Agent, it only takes less than 1s.
- I'm not sure if the site has a special action, but the length of data in the response looks the same.
- I also try `python requests`, it doesn't have this problem. So, I think this might be an axios bug. 
- I test 2 version of axios `0.27.2` and `1.1.3`, both of them has this problem.
- I also test in both Windows 11 and bullseye-slim container, both of them has this problem.


### To Reproduce

You can use the following code to reproduce it in nodejs.

### Code snippet

```js
const axios = require('axios');

let headers = {
    'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8',
    'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.9,ms;q=0.8,te;q=0.7',
    'Cache-Control': 'max-age=0',
    'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
    'Upgrade-Insecure-Requests': '1',
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko)'
};

let start = process.hrtime.bigint();
axios.get('https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/about', { headers: headers})
.then(res => {
    console.log(`time cost: ${process.hrtime.bigint() - start} ns (without browser in header)`);
    console.log(`content length: ${res.data.length} (without browser in header)`);
});

headers = {
    'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8',
    'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.9,ms;q=0.8,te;q=0.7',
    'Cache-Control': 'max-age=0',
    'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
    'Upgrade-Insecure-Requests': '1',
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/103.0.5060.114'
};
start = process.hrtime.bigint();
axios.get('https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/about', { headers: headers})
.then(res => {
    console.log(`time cost: ${process.hrtime.bigint() - start} ns (with browser in header)`);
    console.log(`content length: ${res.data.length} (with browser in header)`);
});
```


### Expected behavior

The example output is shown as follows.
```
time cost: 573916100 ns (without browser in header)
content length: 182009 (without browser in header)
time cost: 9478154500 ns (with browser in header)
content length: 182066 (with browser in header)
```
When include browser info in User-Agent, the time cost is 10 times more than exclude the browser info.

### Axios Version

0.27.2

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

16.15.1

### OS

debian bullseye slim

_No response_

title: react + axios latest (in internal module) - will always use cjs instead of the esm version
body:
### Describe the bug

if you're building a react project, the default webpack will not inclide cjs files and because there's no way to tell axios of internal modules to use the ESM version, it wont load the module.

the only workaround is to "eject" the configs from the react project and manually change the webpack.config file to include any cjs files and also manually include aliases for any missing dependencies


### To Reproduce

- create react app using react-scripts.
- include a module that uses axios in the latest version (^1.1.3)
- try to invoke any axios using function from that module in your react app -> fails -> the axios imported is a string containing the location of the cjs file instead of the actual module.

title: Decompress not applied for response without content-length header
body:
### Describe the bug

`content-encoding` header is ignored if `content-length` is not set on response. For connect compression middleware, the returned response appears to have `Transfer-Encoding`, `content-encoding`, but no `content-length` header, so axios request returns uncompressed value.

I believe `content-length` is an optional header?

Related to commit: https://github.com/axios/axios/commit/a3d901777bc06e78adae3081f694cdbf76241303



### To Reproduce

_No response_

### Code snippet

```js
const responseLength = +res.headers['content-length'];
...

if ((!responseLength || res.statusCode === 204) && res.headers['content-encoding']) {

}
```

title: Axios @1.2.0 brakes on 128 byte text file
body:
### Describe the bug

In axios@1.2.0 - When the text file size is greater than 127 bytes it returns something like `"\u001f�\b\u0000\u0000..."` but in previous version (tested in 1.1.3) it is returning correct text string.

||Version|File size|Response|
|-|-|-|-|
|✅|1.1.3|127 B|`"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa..."`|
|✅|1.2.0|127 B|`"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa..."`|
|✅|1.1.3|128 B|`"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa..."`|
|❌|1.2.0|128 B|`"\u001f�\b\u0000\u0000\u0000..."`|

### To Reproduce

https://runkit.com/avaogntheme/5fc9c5e149e735001aa7fe37

### Code snippet

```js
const axios113 = require("axios@1.1.3")
const axios120 = require("axios")

const url127 = "https://meimg.b-cdn.net/text-127.txt" // 127 byte txt file
const url128 = "https://meimg.b-cdn.net/text-128.txt" // 128 byte txt file

const options = { responseType: 'text' }


console.log('------------- TESTING WITH 127 BYTES FILE -------------')

console.log('✅ Axios Version 1.1.3')
const axios_113_127b = await axios113.get(url127, options)
console.log(axios_113_127b.data)

console.log('✅ Axios Version 1.2.0')
const axios_120_127b = await axios120.get(url127, options)
console.log(axios_120_127b.data)


console.log('------------- TESTING WITH 128 BYTES FILE -------------')

console.log('✅ Axios Version 1.1.3')
const axios_113_128b = await axios113.get(url128, options)
console.log(axios_113_128b.data)

console.log('❌ Axios Version 1.2.0')
const axios_120_128b = await axios120.get(url128, options)
console.log(axios_120_128b.data)
```


### Expected behavior

Expected to return a valid string such as `"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa..."` 
But it returns something like `"\u001f�\b\u0000\u0000\u0000..."`

### Axios Version

`1.2.0`

### Node.js Version

`18.11.0`

I have a custom Axios instance that uses interceptors to return responses. According to the Axios docs, the success interceptor is called whenever there is a 2xx status and the error interceptor is called if it's any status other than 2xx.  I want to display an error dialog when the error interceptor is called.

The problem: I want to display the error message coming from the API response in the dialog.
```js
const responseErrorInterceptor = async (error: AxiosError): ServiceResult<AxiosError> => {
  if (error.response) {
    store.dispatch(setErrorDialog(undefined, /*display api error response here*/));
```

author: lj-ditrapani

title: Proposal: Simplify response model to avoid `Converting circular structure to JSON` error
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

It is common practice to log every request and response from an application, using JSON.stringify. However, this doesn't work in Axios because the response model is very complex (it contains a copy of the request and cyclical types). I am not the first person to experience this based on the number of issues and StackOverflow questions raised about this:
- https://github.com/axios/axios/issues?q=circular+structure+json
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45319090/axios-gives-me-converting-circular-structure-to-json-error-while-sending-the-dat
- https://github.com/googlemaps/google-maps-services-js/issues/351

It is quite time consuming to research this issue to identify the solution - that only `response.data` is convertable to JSON so the code should be changed to `JSON.stringify(response.data)`. In addition, this is only a subset of the information needed to be logged (e.g. HTTP response code, etc), so for developers to correctly log the Axios response, it is something like:
`JSON.stringify({
   data: response.data,
   status: response.status,
   statusText: response.statusText,
   headers: response.headers
)`

or alternative we could delete the fields within response that don't belong there, namely `config` and `request`, but then that means mutating the response, i.e.

```
delete response.config;
delete response.request;
console.log(JSON.stringify(response))
```

### Describe the solution you'd like

The AxiosResponse should contain only those fields that are part of the response, so I propose that `config` and `request` are removed. It is not the responsibility of the `AxiosResponse` object to contain the corresponding request - if needed, a new object such as `AxiosOperation` can be created to contain the request, response and config.

In addition, the config and request fields should be examined for circular dependencies and the modelling adjusted to avoid them, since it causes unnecessary confusion to have a circular structure.

Obviously this is a massive breaking change, but given the issues that this causes in many downstream libraries and services that use Axios, the severity of this issue should not be understated.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

We could consider creating a custom function to JSONify the response or could try to mitigate this issue via documentation, but I don't believe that the adoption of either option would be easy given the huge number of projects and libraries that use Axios.

### Additional context/Screenshots

_No response_

title: Axios attempts to resolve IPv6 addresses as hostnames
body:
### Describe the bug

Up to v0.27.2, axios could be used to reach ipv6 endpoings with a format like the following: `http://[2001:db8::5]:6001/lists`. As of 1.0.0, axios thinks that's a hostname and attempts to use dns to resolve it. 

```
  cause: Error: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND [2001:db8::5]
      at GetAddrInfoReqWrap.onlookup [as oncomplete] (node:dns:107:26) {
    errno: -3008,
    code: 'ENOTFOUND',
    syscall: 'getaddrinfo',
    hostname: '[2001:db8::5]'
  }
}
```

### To Reproduce

Attempt to make a axios call to a ipv6 address using the common [IPv6]:PORT format.

### Code snippet

```js
let res = await axios.post('http://[2001:db8::5]:6001/lists', {})
```


### Expected behavior

Axios should attempt to open a socket to  2001:db8::5 port 6001 and make the request.

author: abhiseksubham

title: Axios does not append form boundary for multipart requests in node environment
body:
### Describe the bug

When trying to interface with endpoints that consume `multipart/form-data` requests, on non browser applications axios needs to use `form-data` package. By default, axios attempts to send `Content-Type: application/www-form-url-encoded` content type header for non file uploads, and any requests that would attempt to send `form-data` objects. This can be overridden by sending your own `Content-Type` header of `multipart/form-data`. Due to this, it's possible to confuse HTTP servers, because then axios does not include the boundary definition, as it is required by HTTP specification for multipart requests.

Below is the actual request sent by axios
```
POST /api/v1/upload HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:8080
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Content-Length: [length]
User-Agent: axios
Accept: *
Connection: close

---boundary
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="metadata"
Content-Type: application/json

{"name": "foo"}
---boundary
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="content"; filename="foo"
Content-Type: application/octet-stream

[binary content]
---boundary--
```

Workaround for this issue (as per snippet in reproduce) is to rebuild the content-type header yourself, by appending `FormData#getBoundary()` value.

```ts
import FormData from 'form-data'
import axios from 'axios'
import * as fs from 'fs'

const api = axios.create({
    "baseURL": "http://localhost:8080/api/v1"
})
const formData = new FormData();
const metadata = {
    "name": "foo"
};
const file = fs.createReadStream("file.txt");

formData.append('metadata', JSON.stringify(metadata), {contentType: 'application/json'})
formData.append('content', file, {filename: metadata.name});

const responsedata =(await api.post('upload', formData, {
    headers: {'Content-Type': `multipart/form-data; boundary=${formData.getBoundary()}`}
})).data
```

### To Reproduce

The issue can be reproduced by sending a `FormData` object via `axios#post(string, object, object)` method as follows.

### Code snippet

```js
import FormData from 'form-data'
import axios from 'axios'
import * as fs from 'fs'

const api = axios.create({
    "baseURL": "http://localhost:8080/api/v1"
})
const formData = new FormData();
const metadata = {
    "name": "foo"
};
const file = fs.createReadStream("file.txt");

formData.append('metadata', JSON.stringify(metadata), {contentType: 'application/json'})
formData.append('content', file, {filename: metadata.name});

const responsedata =(await api.post('upload', formData, {
    headers: {'Content-Type': `multipart/form-data`}
})).data
```


### Expected behavior

Expected behavior is that the request looks like this in its raw http form

```
POST /api/v1/upload HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:8080
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=---boundary
Content-Length: [length]
User-Agent: axios
Accept: *
Connection: close

---boundary
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="metadata"
Content-Type: application/json

{"name": "foo"}
---boundary
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="content"; filename="foo"
Content-Type: application/octet-stream

[binary content]
---boundary--
```
Notice that content-type header contains boundary definition

### Axios Version

0.24.0

### Adapter Version

?

### Browser

Node specific

### Browser Version

Node specific

### Node.js Version

16.13.2

### OS

WSL2

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
form-data: 4.0.0
```


### Additional context/Screenshots

_No response_

title: WebStreams api is not working with typescript
body:
### Describe the bug

When I fetch data over webstreams api, there is an error in runtime:
TypeError: resp.getReader is not a function
Which is strange, the typescript compiler is not throwing an error, and I can see the data in the chrome`s network panel.

### To Reproduce

Make a get request in React or any client app. There will no data reach the client side.
P.S: I see the data with console.log(resp.data), but I can`t use this as a stream.


### Code snippet

```js
const resp = await axios.get<ReadableStream<Uint8Array>>(url, {          
             responseType: 'stream',      
             signal: abortController.signal     
          });
const reader = resp.data.getReader();
while (true) {
    const { value, done } = await reader.read();
    console.log(value);
}
```


### Expected behavior

I should get data via getReader().

author: OVYVO

title: node:util.types.isNativeError() is false for AxiosError
body:
### Describe the bug

Using [util.types.isNativeError()](https://nodejs.org/docs/latest-v16.x/api/util.html#utiltypesisnativeerrorvalue) with an `AxiosError` returns `false`.
At the same time, `axiosError instanceof Error` returns `true`.

I would consider this a bug of `axios`, because it claims that `class AxiosError extends Error`: https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/f12d01eed10c645679dc2633765041d1c2287eea/index.d.ts#L359
I couldn't figure out why it behaves like that.

### To Reproduce

https://runkit.com/embed/nty2gimaty2q

### Code snippet

```js
var { types } = require('node:util');
var { AxiosError } = require("axios");

console.log(types.isNativeError(new AxiosError())); // false
console.log(new AxiosError() instanceof Error); // true

// at the same time:
class CustomSubError extends Error {}
console.log(types.isNativeError(new CustomSubError())); // true
```


### Expected behavior

`util.types.isNativeError(new AxiosError())` should return `true`.

### Axios Version

1.2.1

author: Brandon-Ln

title: formToJSON should not separate properties with minus character
body:
### Describe the bug

`formToJSON` is a helpful function and I use it when I have a form to submit, but do additional validation on individual properties before sending it to backend. However, when I have a form input with a name like `user-name`, this property is converted to object as `{ user: { name: '' } }` instead of `{ 'user-name': '' }`. I think this is an unexpected behavior.

### To Reproduce

```
var axios = require("axios")
var { JSDOM  } = require("jsdom")
var { FormData } = new JSDOM().window;

const formData = new FormData();
formData.append('user-name', 'johndoe');

const result = axios.formToJSON(formData);
console.log(result);
```

### Code snippet

[Try the above snippet.](https://runkit.com/embed/5iwuam2wfr63)

### Expected behavior

Properties should only be splitted with `.` and `[ ]`. Currently, they are splitted by `-` and ` ` (space) too. 

While it can be argued that `.` and `[ ]` can also be inside a property key, they are less likely than `-` and ` `. As an advanced option, there can be an escape character to allow this case. However this may be an overkill.

### Axios Version

1.2.1

### Adapter Version


### Browser

All browsers

### Browser Version

All versions

### Node.js Version

All versions

All OS

title: Multiple XSRF-TOKEN
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I have 2 cookies from 2 domain:

- `XSRF-TOKEN .domain.com`
- `XSRF-TOKEN .subdomain.domain.com`

`XSRF-TOKEN .domain.com` is sent in `X-XSRF-TOKEN` causing CSRF mismatch

### Describe the solution you'd like

I need to send `XSRF-TOKEN` local for `.subdomain.domain.com`

### Describe alternatives you've considered

`[...document.cookie.matchAll(new RegExp('(^|;\\s*)(XSRF-TOKEN)=([^;]*)', 'g'))]` could match both but has no info to distinguish them

### Additional context/Screenshots

```bash
Probably there is no workaround according to this:
https://stackoverflow.com/a/2959110/9852809
```

title: Broken `AxiosHeaders` typings
body: ### Describe the bug `AxiosHeaders` types makes it so trying to call methods on the instance raises a `typescript` compilation error. `AxiosRequestConfig.headers` can be either `RawAxiosRequestHeaders` or `AxiosHeaders`. `RawAxiosRequestHeaders`, being an alias of `Record` causes methods to not be callable. ### To Reproduce Invoke any method on `AxiosRequestConfig.headers`. ### Code snippet ```js const api = axios.create({ ... }) api.interceptors.request.use(req => { // Trying to access any method on `req.headers` will result in // a "This expression is not callable" TS error req.headers.set('foo', 'bar') }) ``` ### Expected behavior Should be able to call any method declared in `AxiosHeaders`. ### Environment * `axios`: `1.2.2` * `node`: `18.12.1` * `typescript`: `4.9.4` * macOS Ventura `13.1`
author: nfantone

body:

- If you're reporting a bug, ensure it isn't already fixed in the latest axios version.
- If you need a new feature there's a chance it's already implemented in a [library](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/master/ECOSYSTEM.md) or you can implement it using [interceptors](https://github.com/axios/axios#interceptors).
- Don't remove any title of the issue template, or it will be treated as invalid by the bot.

**⚠️👆 Delete the instructions before submitting the issue 👆⚠️**

-->

#### Summary

I'm getting the following error after upgrading axios to version 1.2.2:

> TS2339: Property 'Authorization' does not exist on type 'AxiosHeaders | Partial<RawAxiosHeaders & MethodsHeaders & CommonHeaders>'.
>   Property 'Authorization' does not exist on type 'AxiosHeaders'

The same snippet of code is working on version 1.2.1:

```
import axios from 'axios';

axios.interceptors.request.use((request) => {
    request.headers
        ? (request.headers.Authorization = `Bearer ${token}`)
        : (request.headers = { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` });
});
```

#### Environment
 - Axios 1.2.2
 - Node.js 18.12.1
 - OS: Ubuntu 20.04.4 LTS (WSL2)

title: No error.request.getHeaders() function when ETIMEDOUT occurs
body:
### Describe the bug

Environment: Node.js
Axios version: v1.2.2

---

According to [axios document for error handling](https://axios-http.com/docs/handling_errors), `error.request` is an instance of http.ClientRequest in node.js.
But it seems to be an instance of Writable in ETIMEDOUT error case.

I printed it in normal error(ERR_BAD_REQUEST & 404) case and timeout error case.

* ERR_BAD_REQUEST & 404
  ```
  ex.request: <ref *2> ClientRequest { ... }
  ```
* ETIMEDOUT
  ```
  ex.request: <ref *6> Writable { ... }
  ```

So, I can't call error.request.getHeaders(). It raises `request.getHeaders() is not a function` error.

author: marcossouzadotdev

title: Customizable config merging
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

For more advanced use cases it would be very helpful to be able to customize how config merging happens.  The default `mergeConfig` function just uses the equivalent of `Object.assign` for unknown keys in the config, which makes some things more difficult than they should be.

In the case where I just encountered this I'm trying to include an array of values that should be merged, but for an array the new value just overwrites the old one instead of merging them.

### Describe the solution you'd like

The `mergeConfig` function is already exposed so that you can use it externally. Making it possible to replace that and have Axios use the replaced version is one option. An even easier option would be to export the `mergeMap` that it uses to decide how to merge config keys so that you could add custom properties to that to influence how they get merged.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently what I'm doing to work around this is subclassing `Axios` (with a pretty significant amount of copy-pasted code) just to control how the config gets merged..

### Additional context/Screenshots

I'm willing to put together a PR, I just wanted to bring it up first in case any of the maintainers have strong feelings about which approach to take (or if there is some way to do this already that I've missed).

title: `config.transport` is not documented and its type is not defined
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

While `config.transport` option is implemented ([ref](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/1e58a659ec9b0653f4693508d748caa5a41bb1a2/lib/adapters/http.js#L357-L372)), it's not documented in README. The only reference to it is in some comments to #1175 (e.g. https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/1175#issuecomment-1030817133).

In addition, `AxiosRequestConfig` type definition ([ref](https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/1e58a659ec9b0653f4693508d748caa5a41bb1a2/index.d.ts#L286-L326)) doesn't include that of `transport`.

Is `config.transport` intentionally undocumented?

### Describe the solution you'd like

Otherwise, I'm glad to submit a PR.

### Section/Content To Improve

In the README.md, on the default branch v1.x, in the badges, the 'CI' badge shows with a link to a shields issue rather than an actual CI status.

### Suggested Improvement

Hi,

I saw that the 'CI' badge was broken for axios on the 'default' v1.x branch - it looks like it's #5046 that breaks it on v1.x - v1 and main are both fine.


Update the url to remove the shields issue link and link to the actual CI status.

Currently badge looks like: 

![Screenshot 2023-01-11 at 11 27 33](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20516159/211794902-46a2da8b-7dde-43c9-bb06-9f9037c62441.png)


The shields link explains the issue, but in summary, these badges were changed in a breaking fashion that means they need a url update, so the badges were changed to just link to the github issue rather than showing the data.

Updating the url from:

https://img.shields.io/github/workflow/status/axios/axios/ci?label=CI&logo=github&style=flat-square

to

https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/axios/axios/ci.yml?branch=v1.x&label=CI&logo=github&style=flat-square

solves this (although note that I've specified the branch as v1.x - unclear to me if this is correct, or if it should be main, or something else.

title: Breaking change from 1.1.3 when sending raw data
body:
### Describe the bug

- In 1.1.3, this:
```js
axios.post("/api-endpoint", "lorem")
```
produces a POST request with raw body `lorem` and a `Content-Type` header of `text/plain;charset=UTF-8`.
In 1.2.2, it produces a POST request with body `lorem` but  `Content-Type` header of `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

- In 1.1.3, this:
```js
.post(edpGetAllGroups, search, {
  headers: {
    "Content-Type": "text/plain",
  },
})
```
produces a POST request with raw body `lorem` and a `Content-Type` header of `text/plain;charset=UTF-8` (same).
In 1.2.2, it produces a POST request with body `lorem` but  `Content-Type` header of `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` (it even *ignores* the `Content-Type` directive).

title: using `toFormData` with the right `Content-Type`
body:
### Describe the issue

When sending a request, `Content-Type` of **the files** in the form is set to `application/octet-stream` by default.

### Example Code

```js
import axios from 'axios';
const buffer = ... // download a binary file
                   // it's always a png file
const form = axios.toFormData({
  name: 'pic.png',
  type: 'image',
  content: buffer, // the file field
});
await axios.postForm('https://www.feishu.cn/approval/openapi/v2/file/upload', form, {
  proxy: { // record this request
    protocol: 'http:',
    host: 'localhost',
    port: 8888,
  }
});
```


### Expected behavior

`Content-Type` of the binary stream can be set to a custom value.

### Axios Version

1.2.3

### Describe the issue

Hello. I'm using webpack (symfony encore) and I am having a problem with `buffer` and Axios and `crypto-addr-codec` lib

I first installed crypto-addr-codec and webpack compiled but on browser I would get `ReferenceError: Buffer is not defined` So I added a plugin to webpack like this:

>  Buffer: [stdLibBrowser.buffer, 'Buffer']

Now it works. Later I Installed latest axios and now I am getting a compile issue:

```
Module build failed: Module not found:
"./node_modules/axios/lib/helpers/toFormData.js" contains a reference to the file "/Users/joshlopes/work/project/node_modules/node-stdlib-browser/node_modules/buffer".
This file can not be found, please check it for typos or update it if the file got moved.
```

Removing the Buffer plugin line it works, but fails on browser like the past issue (ReferenceError: Buffer is not defined), renaming it to small `b` (`buffer`) works too but again fails on Browser. Same issue. 

I don't know if it's me or an actual bug with axios. Help please? or a workaround ? Thank you

author: orgads

title: Default exports for browser points to module that imports node modules
body:
### Describe the bug

In `package.json` the "default" condition which is used when the package is brought into a browser build with `import` rather than `require` points to `./index.js`.

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/6600d51e6bbb7db984484ea09f62ec22f9044ed8/package.json#L14

Without any other build tool intervention, this ends up bringing in `./lib/platform/node/index.js` which ends up trying to import built in Node modules like `url`.

To correctly get the browser working version, the end user needs to configure their own build tool to apply the "browsers" substitution from `package.json` and have a plugin for handling the commonjs of the `form-data` package as even with the substitution the import graph ends up bringing in `./lib/env/classes/FormData.js`.

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/6600d51e6bbb7db984484ea09f62ec22f9044ed8/lib/env/classes/FormData.js#L1


### To Reproduce

https://stackblitz.com/edit/node-pgl94q?file=index.js

The above example uses [Web Dev Server](https://modern-web.dev/docs/dev-server/overview/) to serve a bare `index.html` page with a `index.js` written in ESM that imports `axios`. Web Dev Server with the `--node-resolve` command line flag uses `@rollup/plugin-node-resolve` to convert bare module specifiers into paths in `node_module`. Notice this results in the error `Could not resolve import "url".`

### Code snippet

_No response_

### Expected behavior

I think it would make more sense for the default browser export to point to `dist/esm/axios.js`.

### Axios Version

1.2.4

title: Post with maxRedirects set to zero still does redirects without callbacks.
body:
### Describe the bug

Performing a post that returns a 302 found response with the following still performs the redirects.
```
client.post("j_security_check", {
                baseURL: loginBaseUrl,
                maxRedirects: 0,
                validateStatus: function (status) {
                    return status == 200 || status == 302;
                }
            });
```

Performing the same operations with `request` and `method:"POST"` does not perform the redirects as is intended.

```
client.request({
                baseURL: loginBaseUrl,
                url: "j_security_check",
                method: "POST",
                withCredentials: true,
                maxRedirects: 0,
                validateStatus: function (status) {
                    return status == 200 || status == 302
                }
            });
```


### To Reproduce

Using NodeJS 16 perform a POST against a service that returns a 302 response with maxRedirects set to zero.  With client.post() the redirects occur, with client.request({method:"POST"}) redirects do not occur as expected.

title: Error: Cannot calculate proper length in synchronous way.
body:
### Describe the bug

Relying on axios automatic serialization to create a form-data results in this error. The following lines are responsible of this error : https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/release/dist/node/axios.cjs#L2654 (mainly the `getLengthSync` function)

According to [form-data themselves](https://github.com/form-data/form-data), using `getLengthSync` is [not recommended](https://github.com/form-data/form-data/blob/master/Readme.md?plain=1#L350).

### To Reproduce

- Create a file buffer with `fs.createReadStream`
- Set HTTP header `Content-Type` to `multipart/form-data` to make use of [automatic serialization](https://github.com/axios/axios#-automatic-serialization-to-formdata)
- Get the form-data `Cannot calculate proper length in synchronous way.` error

### Code snippet

```js
const axios = require("axios");
const fs = require("node:fs");
const fileBuffer = fs.createReadStream("./file.ext");

axios.post('https://httpbin.org/post', {file: fileBuffer}, {
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data'
  }
})
.then(({data}) => console.log(data))
.catch((err) => console.error(err));
```


### Expected behavior

The content length should be calculated without throwing any error.

### Axios Version

1.3.0

title: Posting formdata may result in request header 'Content-Length': 'NaN' being set
body:
### Describe the bug

Passing a [FormData](https://github.com/form-data/form-data) object to Axios client may result in request header 'Content-Length': 'NaN' being set.

### To Reproduce

Given the following code:

```ts
// client is an Axios instance
const pdf = await client.get(pdfUrl, { responseType: 'stream' });

const form = new FormData();
form.append('file', pdf.data, { filename, contentType: 'application/pdf' });

const res = await client.post(urlToUploadService, form);
```

We get the following request header added:

```
    headers: AxiosHeaders {
      Accept: 'application/json, text/plain, */*',
      'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data; boundary=--------------------------852062351748726458865935',
      'User-Agent': 'axios/1.3.2',
      'Content-Length': 'NaN',
      'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, compress, deflate, br'
    },
```

I believe `'Content-Length': 'NaN',` is not something we want :) I'm pretty sure the header was not added with Axios version 1.2.6. 

---------

I believe this is related code:

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/0b449293fc238f30f39ab9ed0fca86a23c8a6a79/lib/adapters/http.js#L266-L267


If we log form's getLength:

```
  form.getLength((err, length) => {
    console.log(err, length);
  }),
```

We'll get a hint of what is going on:

```
null NaN
```

### Axios Version

1.3.2

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

v18.14.0

### OS

macos 13.2

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

- I believe https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/5521 is relevant as it tried to fix some related issue(?).
- Not 100% sure if this is a https://github.com/form-data/form-data issue or an Axios issue, but given it previously worked I though I would register it here.

title: Not very appropriate error tip when set "adapter: 'http'" in browser env.
body: ### Describe the bug Hey. As stated in the title, when i set "adapter: 'http'" in the request config options with chrome browser, the console tells me: `Error: Adapter 'http' is not available in the build`. But in the source code, the correct tip should be `Error: Adapter 'http' is not supported by the environment`. The reason for this problem is that, `httpAdapter`'s value is `null` instead of `false` in browser environment. The `browser` field in the package.json has extra configuration. ### To Reproduce You can try it in [codepen](https://codepen.io/code1994/pen/dyqvaVx?editors=1111) ### Code snippet ```js // the test code axios('/api', { adapter: 'http' }) // package.json in axios { "browser": { "./lib/adapters/http.js": "./lib/helpers/null.js" } } ``` ### Expected behavior when set `adapter: 'http'` in the request config options, the console should be `Error: Adapter 'http' is not supported by the environment`. ### Axios Version 1.2.1 ### Adapter Version _No response_ ### Browser _No response_ ### Browser Version _No response_ ### Node.js Version _No response_ ### OS _No response_ ### Additional Library Versions _No response_ ### Additional context/Screenshots

title: Network Error / unsupported protocol on 302 redirect with custom scheme
body:
### Describe the bug

Axios request fails on a 302 status code when a custom scheme / protocol is in the location header.

On react-native, it gives a NETWORK_ERROR instead of a unsupported protocol.

This is related to https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/4901.

### To Reproduce

Call an endpoint with axios that returns a 302 with a Location header that is not a supported protocol by axios, like smartbills://

### Code snippet

```js
var axios = require("axios")


axios.get("https://axios.free.beeceptor.com/scheme", {maxRedirect:0}).then(() => console.log("success")).catch((error) => {
console.error(error.request)
throw error;
});
```


### Expected behavior

I don't except the client to crash because the server response is valid. I should be able to handle the redirect as I wish.

As we use it for OAuth2, any client can pass their own app scheme, so we need to be able to detect it and redirect the user to the client app.

### Axios Version

1.3.4

author: doxroot

title: Request interceptor can not set header with name contains digits.
body:
### Describe the bug

I'm trying to add a request interceptor with the following code:

```typescript
  myInterceptor = (config: InternalAxiosRequestConfig): InternalAxiosRequestConfig => {    
    config.headers.set('a1', 'value');
    return config;
  };
```
It turns out is has no effect.

I believe it is due to the incorrect `isValidHeaderName`  function https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js#L32-L34:

```
function isValidHeaderName(str) {
  return /^[-_a-zA-Z]+$/.test(str.trim());
}
```

And been called with https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js#L103-L107

```
    } else if(utils.isString(header) && (header = header.trim()) && !isValidHeaderName(header)) {
      setHeaders(parseHeaders(header), valueOrRewrite);
    } else {
      header != null && setHeader(valueOrRewrite, header, rewrite);
    }
```

Because `isValidHeaderName(header)` returns `false`, so it goes to `setHeaders(parseHeaders(header), valueOrRewrite);` logic where `parseHeaders(header)` gets nothing form `a1` string.

Actually `isValidHeaderName(header)` should return `true` for `a1` and goto the else branch:
```
header != null && setHeader(valueOrRewrite, header, rewrite);
```
which will set the `a1` header correctly.

----
Accroding to rfc9110:
- https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-fields
- https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-tokens
digits are allowed in header names.


### To Reproduce

Run the below code here: https://runkit.com/embed/3ic1gajcu2yn

### Code snippet

```js
const {AxiosHeaders} = require("axios")

const headers = new AxiosHeaders();
headers.set('a1', 'value')
headers.get('a1') // -> undefined
```


### Expected behavior

Expected the header been successfully set. And headers.get('a1') returns `value`.

### Axios Version

1.3.4

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

14

### OS

_No response_

### Additional Library Versions

_No response_

### Additional context/Screenshots

_No response_

title: Refresh token flow
body:
### Describe the issue

Hi, I am developing a web application and using axios to send and receive data. In this project, the token flow authentication system is updated and works well. But when the access token expires, first it shows xhr.js not allowed error, then it sends token update request, new tokens are received and new data is received with new tokens. Is there a way to prevent the xhr.js 401 error from showing the console?

### Example Code

```js
​axiosInstance​.​interceptors​.​response​.​use​( 
  
 ​    ​// return response 
 ​    ​response​ ​=>​ ​response​, 
  
 ​    ​/** 
 ​     * error function 
 ​     *  
 ​     * ​@param​ error  
 ​     * ​@returns​  
 ​     */ 
 ​    ​(​error​: ​any​)​ ​=>​ ​{ 
  
 ​        ​// get prevoius request config details 
 ​        ​const​ ​prevRequest​ ​=​ ​error​.​config​; 
  
 ​        ​/** 
 ​         * check response status and check if prevoius _retry property is false 
 ​         */ 
 ​        ​if​ ​(​error​.​response​?.​status​ ​===​ ​401​ ​&&​ ​!​prevRequest​.​_retry​)​ ​{ 
  
 ​            ​// set _retry to true 
 ​            ​prevRequest​.​_retry​ ​=​ ​true​; 
  
 ​            ​/** 
 ​             * return axiosInstance for send post request to api/v1/tokens  
 ​             * with refresh token data 
 ​             */ 
 ​            ​return​ ​axios 
 ​                ​.​post​(​'/tokens'​,​ ​{​ ​_ref​: ​store​.​getState​(​)​.​user​.​data​?.​refresh_token​ ​}​) 
 ​                ​.​then​(​response​ ​=>​ ​{ 
  
 ​                    ​// get access token 
 ​                    ​const​ ​{​ access_token ​}​ ​=​ ​response​.​data​.​user​; 
  
 ​                    ​// set new user data in redux 
 ​                    ​store​.​dispatch​(​addUser​(​{ 
  
 ​                        ​// set user name from redux value 
 ​                        ​username​: ​store​.​getState​(​)​.​user​.​data​?.​username​ ​as​ ​string​, 
  
 ​                        ​// set avatar from redux value 
 ​                        ​avatar​: ​store​.​getState​(​)​.​user​.​data​?.​avatar​ ​as​ ​string​, 
  
 ​                        ​// set refresh token from response 
 ​                        ​refresh_token​: ​response​.​data​.​user​.​refresh_token​, 
  
 ​                        ​// set access token from response 
 ​                        ​access_token​: ​access_token 
 ​                    ​}​)​)​; 
  
 ​                    ​// set access token to headere prevRequest 
 ​                    ​prevRequest​.​headers​.​Authorization​ ​=​ ​`Bearer ​${​access_token​}​`​; 
 ​                    
 ​                    ​// return axiosInstance with prevRequest  
 ​                    ​return​ ​axios​(​prevRequest​)​; 
 ​                ​}​) 
 ​                ​// if have and error 
 ​                ​.​catch​(​error​ ​=>​ ​{ 
  
 ​                    ​// dispatch user to undefiend 
 ​                    ​store​.​dispatch​(​addUser​(​undefined​)​)​; 
  
 ​                    ​// return error 
 ​                    ​return​ ​Promise​.​reject​(​error​)​; 
  
 ​                ​}​)​; 
 ​        ​} 
  
 ​        ​// return error 
 ​        ​return​ ​Promise​.​reject​(​error​)​; 
 ​    ​} 
 ​)​;
```

title: Authorization Header Stripped in Latest Firefox Developer Edition
body:
### Describe the issue

Using the latest Firefox Developer Edition (111.0b8), & Axios 0.2.7, Authorization headers set in an interceptor are not being sent with the request. This does not occur in Chrome or regular stable Firefox. Doing some research, I found that changing these new settings in `about:config` makes it work again. 

```
network.fetch.redirect.stripAuthHeader = false
network.http.redirect.stripAuthHeader = false
```

Specifically, setting `network.fetch.redirect.stripAuthHeader = false` makes it work for me again, the other setting has no effect.

It seems Firefox has implemented a new spec for the Fetch protocol which requires stripping the Authorization header in certain circumstances, & their change was too aggressive & a patch was added, but I'm still getting the problem with the patched version. Here is where I found the new settings & new behavior: https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1817980

Supposedly , the stripping only occurs when the browser receives a redirect to a different origin response back from the server. However, that is not the case in my situation, I get a 200 response back, & the SPA using Axios & the API server Axios is calling are on the same subdomain & https protocol, so there should be no cross-origin issues.

Anybody know how to make Authorization headers set in Axios interceptors work with this new behavior?

### Example Code

_No response_

_No response_

### Axios Version

0.2.7

### Describe the bug

In my app i upgrade axios from ^0.21.4 to 1.3.3 

axios.create is not a function but when i did make it axios.default.create it started working what should be correct way to use it.

axios.create still works fine in nodeJS app
but in browser i see axios.create is not a function 

### To Reproduce

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62964295/226691622-5d370c0c-2b01-4f26-9d0c-c938ed43c221.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62964295/226691872-874b25e9-190c-4e31-b288-6ed2367b7e35.png)


### Code snippet


title: Unclear error message is thrown when specifying an empty proxy authorization
body:
### Describe the bug

Currently, if we specify an empty proxy authorization, e.g. `proxy.auth = {username: "", password: ""}`, an unclear error message is thrown.

```
TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE]: The first argument must be of type string or an instance of Buffer, ArrayBuffer, or Array or an Array-like Object. Received an instance of Object
    at new NodeError (node:internal/errors:399:5)
    at Function.from (node:buffer:334:9)
    at setProxy (file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:93:10)
    at dispatchHttpRequest (file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:390:7)
    at file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:142:5
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at wrapAsync (file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:122:10)
    at http (file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/adapters/http.js:148:10)
    at Axios.dispatchRequest (file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/core/dispatchRequest.js:51:10)
    at Axios.request (file:///axios-with-empty-authn/node_modules/axios/lib/core/Axios.js:142:33) {
  code: 'ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE'
}
```

Seem this error happened from this PR: [#4436](https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/4436/files#diff-586c04c24608b86a074073c5514cc195be5c4c87ddb56da233f6d998ada4159fR43-R45)

https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/2e70cecda42993e1153248f0f96715c3c55f7f39/lib/adapters/http.js#L87-L96

The cause of the error is `proxy.auth` is still an object, not a string. Therefore, `Buffer.from` throws the error because It does not accept an object.

### To Reproduce

Run the code snippet

### Code snippet

```js
// index.mjs
import axios from "axios";

const proxy = {
  protocol: "http:",
  host: "hostname.abc.xyz",
  port: 3300,
  auth: {
    username: "",
    password: "",
  }
};

await axios.get("https://test-domain.abc", {proxy});
```


### Expected behavior

I'm not sure whether this is expected behavior or not. So, I suggest two options below

### Expected behavior 01

Show a clear message about empty proxy authorization.

For example: `Invalid proxy authorization`

### Expected behavior 02

Ignore the empty proxy authorization and skip the basic proxy authorization.

### Axios Version

1.3.4

_No response_

### Node.js Version

18.14.2

However, during the deployment on Vercel, I keep encountering the following error related to Axios: "SyntaxError: Unexpected token (705:2) in /vercel/path0/node_modules/axios/lib/utils.js." I want to resolve this error. I have tried the following steps to fix the error: 1. Set "strict" to "false" and "true" in tsconfig.json. 2. title: AxiosError.prototype.toJSON cannot be stringify in JEST
body: ### Describe the bug I get the error when run Jest for test. It is because the `toJSON` function is a property at the prototype of `AxiosError` https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/core/AxiosError.js#L33 and the `jest` will remove the prototype at https://github.com/facebook/jest/blob/main/packages/jest-util/src/deepCyclicCopy.ts#L31. so the `AxiosError` cannot be `stringify` because the `__currentRequest.req` is a circular structure but `Axios@0.26.x` is work fine, because the `toJSON` is the property of `AxiosError` https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v0.26.1/lib/core/enhanceError.js#L13 ### To Reproduce 1. make a http request error use axios 2. run test with `--watch` flag ### Code snippet _No response_ ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Axios Version latest ### Adapter Version http ### Browser _No response_ ### Browser Version _No response_ ### Node.js Version 16.x ### OS _No response_ ### Additional Library Versions ```bash jest@29 ``` ### Additional context/Screenshots _No response_

title: What is the rationale for defining a second paramter between Response and Request type?
body:
### Describe the issue

The post method has the following type:

```typescript
post<T = any, R = AxiosResponse<T>, D = any>(url: string, data?: D, config?: AxiosRequestConfig<D>): Promise<R>;
```

The infered type ` R = AxiosResponse<T>` must be the 3rd paramter since the `T` (Response Type) and `D` (Request Parameters Type) are sufficient to fix the fully determine.

### Example Code

Currently, if we wish to define the body data type as well, we have to define 3 types. The 2nd parameter should be infered

```typescript
 axios.post<PostResponse, AxiosResponse<PostResponse> , PostBody>("/api/auth/login", { email, password })
      .then(({ data: user }) => console.log(user))
```


### Expected behavior

The ordering of the paramters must change:

```typescript
post<T = any, D = any, R = AxiosResponse<T>>(url: string, data?: D, config?: AxiosRequestConfig<D>): Promise<R>;
```

so that only two types are required to strongly fix the type and ignore the infered type.

```typescript
 axios.post<PostResponse, PostBody>("/api/auth/login", { email, password })
      .then(({ data: user }) => console.log(user))
```

### Axios Version

title: Can anyone confirm this Axios memory leak.
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/axios/axios/discussions/5640
Originally posted by **ekerner-com** April 9, 2023 I observed this in Chrome on Mac (so Safari essentially). I haven't tested in any other environment. **Every call with Axois adds 4 event listeners and they never get cleaned up**. This inflates memory consumption over time and eventually causes a mem crash. **To reproduce** Leave a page open with an interval running an Axios call every n seconds. **To observe** Open the performance monitor and make sure that the JS Heap and Event Listeners metrics are selected. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5393913/230735923-88c82d5d-dc69-4007-8c31-c2e36794f590.png) I tried the latest and older versions with the same result.

title: Under what specific circumstances will the second function of interceptors. request. use be executed?
body:
### Describe the issue

Under what specific circumstances will the second function of interceptors. request. use be executed?

onerror onabort Will enter the response interceptor，Will not enter the second function of the request interceptor
For example, browser disconnection or Axis cancellation request

The answer I want is, what are the usage scenarios for the second function of the request interceptor?

My current conclusion：Can only multiple request interceptors throw errors in the previous function before being caught by the second function of the next request interceptor?

thanks！！！




### Example Code

```js
axios.interceptors.request.use(function (config) {
  return config;
}, function (error) {
  // TODO：What to do with request errors and what scenarios will trigger this?
  return Promise.reject(error);
});
```

author: PizzaJu

title: Request interceptors are called in revers order
body:
### Describe the bug

Success and error response interceptors are called in the order they were added (as written in the [docs](https://www.npmjs.com/package/axios#multiple-interceptors)), but response interceptors are called in revers order (the last added is called first).


### To Reproduce

https://runkit.com/mtnt/643d3b03c218df000875957b

### Code snippet

```
axios.interceptors.request.use((data) => {
  console.log('request 0');
  
  return data
})

axios.interceptors.request.use((data) => {
  console.log('request 1');
  
  return data
})


axios.interceptors.response.use((data) => {
  console.log('response 0');
  
  return data
})
axios.interceptors.response.use((data) => {
  console.log('response 1');
  
  return data
})


axios.get('https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/axios/1.3.5/axios.min.js')
```

### Expected behavior

There should be consoled:
request 0
request 1
response 0
response 1

### Axios Version

1.3.5

### Adapter Version

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_

### Browser Version

_No response_

### Node.js Version

_No response_

### OS

iOS 16

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
RN 0.71
```


### Additional context/Screenshots

```bash
I found it at v0.27 and take an update to 1.3.5 expecting fix. But it still alive.
It can be reproduced at RN and browser (I did not check node)
```

title: Timeout is not applied
body:
### Describe the issue

Hi All,

I sent POST requests from service running in Cloud run service to GCP Internal Load balancer. The pictorial representation is as given below.   

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38481564/234759016-a18a7b24-f278-4b1b-8b76-2b74b0de523f.png)

Most of the time it works fine without any issues, however in some of the cases the `axios` waits indefinitely without getting timed out even though I have mentioned the timeout for each requests posted.

Upon analyzing the logs, it was found that the response was generated from load balancer end, however it was not reached the cloud run service. And axios waited indefinitely without getting timed out after 100 seconds.   

Could you please help me understand, what could be the potential issue and how this could be resolved?  

### Example Code

```js
try {
   let response = await axios.post(LB_URL, data, {timeout : 100 * 1000});
} catch (err) {
   console.log("error : ", err);
}
```


### Expected behavior

The axios POST request should have got timed out after waiting for 100 seconds. 

### Axios Version

1.3.6

title: axios.delete ignores body
body:
### Describe the bug

When calling `axios.delete`, regardless of whether I put the 2nd parameter as `{ data: { "something" } }`, `{ data: "something" }`, `{ data: { data: "something" }`, the `req.body` will always be `{}`. To be honest, I'm not sure whether it's a next.js bug (due to parser) or axios bug. 

### To Reproduce

on client side:
await axios.delete("/api/favorite", { data: { movieId } }); 

on server side:
const { movieId } = req.body;
console.log(req.body}; // it will print {}
console.log(movieId);  // it will print undefined

### Code snippet

```js
await axios.delete("/api/favorite", { data: { movieId } });
```


### Expected behavior

body should not be empty. POST works even without specifying data.

1.3.6

I have a script at Discord that receives messages in one channel and sends them to another via webhook, using Axios. The problem is that it manages to do this only when the message is without an image.

When there is image, it gives the following error:
`AxiosError: Request failed with status code 400`

I activated the console.log to see if it was receiving the URL and Name variables and it is.
`AxiosError: Request failed with status code 400`

The problem is in this line:
`files: [{url: attachments[0].url, filename: attachments[0].name}]`

Does anyone know how to resolve? Thanks! 


### Example Code

```js
const webhookFr = 'https://discord.com/api/webhooks/1101899486201841.../SjzEplc43SamDHuCTsKFHMSe3fh7uRxjLC-BmVlsJ175ByJ-zf2krSQdsEY0hhhSk...';

function whfr(message, nick, avatarURL, attachments) {
  console.log('-');
  console.log(`Message: ${message}`);
  console.log(`User: ${nick}`);
  console.log(`Avatar: ${avatarURL}`);
    console.log(`Link: ${attachments[0].url}`);
    console.log(`Name: ${attachments[0].name}`);

      .then(response => console.log(':heavy_check_mark: Sent!'))
```

author: codekathiravan

title: Add adapter for electron's `net` module
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

When using axios from electron's main process, the system's proxy settings and trusted certificates are not respected.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Electron exposes a `net` module that allows the main process to make HTTP(S) requests using Chromium's network stack, which integrates much better with the system settings. It has almost the same API as node's `http`/`https`. It also has redirect handling, so the axios adapter won't need to use `follow-redirects`.

I came up with this code to determine if the `net` module is present, but I'm not planning on developing support for this myself. As written, it should never throw an exception, regardless of the contents of `process.versions['electron']`.

```js
// The net module was introduced in Electron versions 1.4.5 (released in November 2016). For code
// simplicity, we'll just check if we're using Electron 2.0 or later (released in May 2018).
const isNetAdapterSupported =
    process !== undefined
    && typeof process !== 'undefined'
    && utils.kindOf(process) === 'process'
    && process.versions['electron'] !== undefined
    && parseInt(process.versions['electron'].split('.')[0]) >= 2;
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Until this is implemented, I'll have to use `net` manually, with the clunky API that comes with that.

### Additional context/Screenshots

### Describe the bug

![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/13010755/cf802972-366b-4101-9608-35ec13b17732)

When I execute the Karma test using the latest release, version 1.4.0, without making any modifications, the test fails as described above.

The test involves sending a POST request from the httpbin side and verifying the expected response. However, when I sent the same request as the test code to Postman(API Test Client), the response was successful, but it took more than 20 seconds to receive a response. Considering the possibility of poor performance in the httpbin service, it might be beneficial to consider replacing it with an alternative service.

title: axios should not use JSON.parse to parse the response when the responseType is set to 'text'
body:
### Describe the bug

When the response is a string, axios parses responses using JSON.parse as the default transformResponse (https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/16b5718954d88fbefe17f0b91101d742b63209c7/lib/defaults.js#L57-L65). JSON.parse tries to make the value a Number (https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/2919), e.g. JSON.parse('646686880670135092987843') => 6.466868806701352e+23. This will cause unexpected result from the client side. 

When client set responseType is to 'text', the axios should not use JSON.parse. Instead, it should return the string directly.

### To Reproduce

_No response_

title: Why do we need to replace `:`, `$`, `,`, `+`, `[`, and `]` back
body:
### Describe the issue


https://github.com/axios/axios/blob/v1.x/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L14

Hi, I'm confused about the encode behavior, why is this necessary?

### Example Code

```js
/**
 * It replaces all instances of the characters `:`, `$`, `,`, `+`, `[`, and `]` with their
 * URI encoded counterparts
 *
 * @param {string} val The value to be encoded.
 *
 * @returns {string} The encoded value.
 */
function encode(val) {
  return encodeURIComponent(val).
    replace(/%3A/gi, ':').
    replace(/%24/g, '$').
    replace(/%2C/gi, ',').
    replace(/%20/g, '+').
    replace(/%5B/gi, '[').
    replace(/%5D/gi, ']');
}
```

```
[ 'a=1; Path=/' ]

User-Agent: axios/1.4.0

Host: httpbin.org
Connection: close
```

```js
import axios from 'axios';

const url = 'https://httpbin.org/cookies/set?a=1';
```

body:
### Describe the bug

Source of problem 
 platform.isStandardBrowserEnv || platform.isStandardBrowserWebWorkerEnv is comming as true because the emulator runs the react native app on some kind of web worker thread 
 bleow code is from  axios/lib/adapters/xhr.js file 
 
  
 
```
 platform.isStandardBrowserEnv = false
 platform.isStandardBrowserWebWorkerEnv = true 
```
`if (utils.isFormData(requestData)) {
      if (platform.isStandardBrowserEnv || platform.isStandardBrowserWebWorkerEnv) {
        requestHeaders.setContentType(false); // Let the browser set it
      } else {
        requestHeaders.setContentType('multipart/form-data;', false); // mobile/desktop app frameworks
      }
    }`
    
    
    
    The solution cloud be to alow the user to set the platform if necessary with Axios.create or something better.

### To Reproduce

try to submit a form data from latest version of axios with react native app running on emulator

### Code snippet

```js
if (utils.isFormData(requestData)) {
      if (platform.isStandardBrowserEnv || platform.isStandardBrowserWebWorkerEnv) {
        requestHeaders.setContentType(false); // Let the browser set it
      } else {
        requestHeaders.setContentType('multipart/form-data;', false); // mobile/desktop app frameworks
      }
    }
```


### Expected behavior

Sending a request to a specific link that in response, one of his header names is "Set" will cause an "uncaughtException" that cannot be avoided, even by using interceptors, since the `setHeader` called before the interceptors for the response handling.

```
(/app/node_modules/axios/dist/node/axios.cjs:1651:30)\n    at /app/node_modules/axios/dist/node/axios.cjs:1656:51\n    at
```

author: clizSec

title: ParamsSerializerOptions contains type definitions that are unnecessary from an implementation standpoint.
body:
### Describe the bug

ParamsSerializerOptions contains type definitions that are unnecessary from an implementation standpoint.

https://github.com/DigitalBrainJS/axios/blob/92f0758429541b05c614dc25bc712379d8a41dbd/lib/helpers/buildURL.js#L51-L57

Under the current situation where documentation is not well maintained, mistakes and misunderstandings can occur with the following argument types.
Check the following examples.

```
axios.create({
  paramsSerializer: {
    encode: value => value
    serialize: value => value // Value not referenced at runtime
  }
})
```

So we should remove the pattern of receiving both functions by modifying the type.
Or you should update the documentation.

PR: https://github.com/axios/axios/pull/5770

body: ### Describe the bug After updating react-native in the application to version 0.71.1 and targetSDK to version 33, a problem began to appear when loading pictures. Moreover, the loading is strange, the first picture does not go away, an error comes. Following it, I send the next one - the picture is sent without problems. I noticed that the bug is observed on Android versions below 12 (tested on this device) The image is attached to formData and sent as a POST request to the server. Memo used, used the XMLHttpRequest class separately. Flipper is not used in the project, so many of the upgrade tips are out of date. It is not an issue with the API. ### To Reproduce _No response_ ### Code snippet ```js const xhrRequest = (data) => { const { accessToken, fileName, type, uri } = data const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest() const body = new FormData() xhr.open('POST', `${URL}${ENDPOINTS.ATTACH_FILE}`) xhr.setRequestHeader('Authorization', `Bearer ${accessToken}`) xhr.setRequestHeader('Content-type', 'multipart/form-data') body.append('file', { uri, name: fileName, type }) xhr.send(body) } export const attachFile = ({ accessToken, fileName, type, uri }) => { const body = new FormData() body.append('file', { name: fileName, uri: Platform.OS === 'android' ? uri : uri.replace('file://', ''), type, }) if (Platform.OS === 'android') { xhrRequest({accessToken, fileName, type, uri}) } return api .post(ENDPOINTS.ATTACH_FILE, body, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}`, 'Content-type': 'multipart/form-data', }, maxContentLength: Infinity, }) .then(R.prop('data')) } ``` ### Expected behavior _No response_ ### Axios Version 0.24.0 ### Adapter Version _No response_ ### Browser _No response_ ### Browser Version _No response_ ### Node.js Version 18.16.1 ### OS _No response_ ### Additional Library Versions ```bash react: 18.2.0, react-native: 0.71.10 ``` ### Additional context/Screenshots _No response_

title: connect EINVAL on requests to locally configured hostname
body:
### Describe the bug

When making a request to the locally configured FQDN (i.e. when the server is making a request to itself), the hostname resolves to the server's link local addresses. When this is an IPv6 address, the request fails. For example, from my server:

```
> dns.lookup('dead10ck.dev', {}, (err, address, family) => console.log('address: %j family: IPv%s', address, family));
GetAddrInfoReqWrap {
  callback: [Function (anonymous)],
  family: 0,
  hostname: 'dead10ck.dev',
  oncomplete: [Function: onlookup]
}
> address: "fe80::62f0:705:bdbd:6279" family: IPv6
```

```
> axios.get('https://dead10ck.dev')
...
Error: connect EINVAL fe80::62f0:705:bdbd:6279:443 - Local (:::0)
      at internalConnect (node:net:1066:16)
      at defaultTriggerAsyncIdScope (node:internal/async_hooks:464:18)
```

title: The axios always try ipv6 first when send request to http://localhost:8800，even if I set family to 4
body:
### Describe the bug

The axios always try ipv6 first when send request to http://localhost:8800，even if I set family to 4.

My server does not listen to ipv6 by default.
So, this issue will cause a long initial connection time(about 300ms).

### To Reproduce

1. start a http server that does not listen on ipv6:
this is a poco http server for c++
```c++
  Poco::Net::HTTPServerParams *params = new Poco::Net::HTTPServerParams;
  params->setMaxQueued(100);
  params->setMaxThreads(16);

  Poco::Net::ServerSocket socket(Poco::Net::SocketAddress("localhost", port_));
  Poco::Net::HTTPServer server(new RequestHandlerFactory(), socket, params);
  server.start();
```
2. send any request to the server use localhost:
```ts
const axios_instance = axios.create({
  baseURL: 'http://localhost:8800',
  family: 4,
  timeout: 1000,
});

axios_instance.post("/project/-/open", {
      path: path
});
```
3. check the initial connection from DevTools
4. capture the tcp traffic:
use localhost
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/31911231/61389e68-268b-4dc1-a566-c707c2212c78)
use 127.0.0.1
![image](https://github.com/axios/axios/assets/31911231/b035428a-1f5a-451a-abec-5c6f2ac3c020)

send request to localhost over ipv4 only(or first) when set the family to 4.

author: Aostas

title: Axios GET - missing bytes downloading raw binary zip data
body:
### Describe the issue

I have an endpoint which returns binary raw zip data. When I consume this endpoint via Postman and "Save to file", the downloaded zip file gets extracted just fine no issues. If I hit the endpoint on browser as well it downloads the zip & extracts with no issues. So, there is no issue with the binary raw data itself.

BUT when I try to download via Axios, I get below error while extracting the zip file. After a lot of digging, I've narrowed it down to some bytes being missing (Corrupted zip: missing 26 bytes) for some reason which points to me not using the right Axios config to properly transform, encode the binary data.

I've been trying all possible config changes (responseType as stream/arraybuffer/blob, responseEncoding as null/utf8/binary etc) still doesn't seem to fix the corrupt file!

Whenever I try to unzip the file, I get the error:
> Unable to expand "test.zip" (Error 79 - Inappropriate file type of format)

If I set the header with Content-Length, then I get this error while unzipping:
> Unable to expand "test.zip" (Error 1 - No such process)

PS: I have another endpoint which returns raw binary PDF data and Axios downloads that perfectly fine! Its just an issue with raw binary ZIP data by the looks of it.

### Example Code

```js
// handling the axios request
getZipFile: ({}) => Axios.get('/zipfile, { params: {...} }, {
  responseType: 'arraybuffer',
  responseEncoding: null,
  maxContentLength: 20000,
  transformResponse: data => data,
})

// handling the axios response
const { data, headers = {} } = await getZipFile({})
console.log(data) // returns above corrupted raw binary zip data

res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/zip');
res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', 'attachment; filename="test.zip"');
return res.end(data);
```


### Expected behavior

I would expect Axios to behave the same way as Postman or browser .ie. download the zip file without any corruption.

### Axios Version

1.3.5

### Adapter Version

HTTP

### Browser

Chrome

### Browser Version

Version 114.0.5735.198 (Official Build) (x86_64)

### Node.js Version

17.9.0

### OS

OSX 13.4.1 (Intel chip)

### Additional Library Versions

```bash
Express 4.18.2
```


### Additional context/Screenshots

_No response_
